title: Security Engineer
description:
**WHO WE ARE** 





 At Trustly, we're building a smarter, faster, and more secure financial future by revolutionizing the world of payments. As a global leader in Open Banking Payments, we are establishing Pay by Bank as the new standard at checkout, providing unparalleled freedom, speed, and ease to millions of consumers and merchants worldwide.
 




  






 Our Ambition: To build the world’s most disruptive payment network and redefine what the payment experience should feel like.
 




  






 Trustly is a global team of innovators, collaborators, and doers.  If you are driven by a strong sense of purpose and thrive in a dynamic, entrepreneurial, and high-growth environment, join us and be part of a team that’s transforming the way the world pays.
 





  




**What you'll do** 
  

  
* Own and continuously improve our vulnerability management program, ensuring full coverage and accurate exposure visibility across all assets
  
* Build and maintain automation around security tooling to ensure data quality, consistency, and actionable insights
  
* Perform security assessments across the SDLC: design reviews, threat modeling, code reviews, and dynamic testing, working closely with engineering teams
  
* Integrate and enforce security controls within CI/CD pipelines (SAST, DAST, secrets detection, dependency scanning), with a strong focus on developer experience
  
* Conduct internal offensive security activities (penetration testing, red teaming, exploitation) to validate real-world risk and identify control gaps
  
* Translate vulnerabilities into real business risk by validating exploitability and prioritizing remediation based on impact
  
* Contribute to incident response and security investigations, including root cause analysis and improvement of detection and response capabilities
  
* Actively participate in improving our security posture by challenging assumptions, refining detection logic, and improving how we measure exposure
  
* Collaborate with infrastructure and platform teams (AWS, Kubernetes, IAM) to ensure secure-by-design architectures
  
* Contribute to threat intelligence efforts by identifying relevant threats and mapping them to our internal stack and exposure
  
* Support the evolution of our security practices, tooling, and processes as we continue to scale our business and security capabilities
  

  


  


  




**Who you are** 
  

  
* You have hands-on experience in cybersecurity engineering, application security, or infrastructure security
  
* Strong understanding of modern cloud environments (preferably AWS), including networking, IAM, and containerized workloads
  
* Experience with vulnerability management and security tooling, with a good understanding of asset exposure and data accuracy
  
* Familiar with integrating security controls into CI/CD pipelines (SAST, DAST, secrets detection, dependency scanning)
  
* Comfortable performing technical security assessments (web, APIs, infrastructure), including validation and exploitation of vulnerabilities
  
* Solid understanding of core security concepts and protocols (DNS, TLS, authentication, CVEs, etc.)
  
* Able to think in terms of risk and prioritize based on real-world impact rather than theoretical vulnerabilities
  
* Experience working in regulated environments such as financial services is a strong plus
  
* Strong collaboration and communication skills, able to work closely with developers and infrastructure teams
  
* Pragmatic mindset, able to balance security requirements with engineering constraints
  
* Curious and proactive, not afraid to challenge existing setups and improve them
  
* Certifications (e.g. OSCP, OSWE or similar) are a plus but not required
  

  


  





  




  


**Our Fantastic Benefits**

 (varies by location)
 




 🌴
 **20 to 30 days of holiday** 
 to support a healthy work-life balance
 



 🥳
 **Monthly team outing allowance** 
 to enjoy social events with your colleagues
 



 👶
 **Parental leave top-up** 
 additional support for new parents
 



 🥐
 **Daily breakfast and on-site perks** 
 to make your workday smoother
 



 💚
 **Well being support** 
 our health allowance covers gym memberships, massages, and much more to help you feel your best
 



**PLUS** 
 additional benefits designed to enhance your work-life experience!
 



  





**Shape Your Role and Make an Impact**




 Join a dynamic environment where you can take ownership, drive change, and continuously embrace new challenges. We’re looking for dedicated and highly motivated individuals who thrive in a fast-paced environment and enjoy collaborating across different areas of the organisation.
 



 If your skills and experience align with this role, we’d love to hear from you!
 **Apply now and submit your CV in English.** 



  


 We may use artificial intelligence (AI) tools to support parts of the hiring process, such as reviewing applications, analyzing resumes, or assessing responses and identifying potential inconsistencies or verification signals in application materials based on available information. These tools assist our recruitment team but do not replace human judgment. Final hiring decisions are ultimately made by humans. If you would like more information about how your data is processed, please contact us.

title: Senior Software Engineer - On Device Machine Learning
description:
**Description & Requirements**
 Electronic Arts creates next-level entertainment experiences that inspire players and fans around the world. Here, everyone is part of the story. Part of a community that connects across the globe. A place where creativity thrives, new perspectives are invited, and ideas matter. A team where everyone makes play happen.
   

  

 Frostbite is EA's proprietary game engine. Its versatile tools and cutting-edge technology power creation on some of the world's most iconic games, including titles from EA SPORTS and Battlefield. By joining Frostbite, you'll be at the forefront of game engine innovation, collaborating with top-tier developers to push the boundaries of interactive realism and immersive gameplay.
   

  

 We are looking for a Senior Software Engineer with expertise in software optimization for gaming consoles and CPU/GPU architectures to join our Machine Learning team. You'll report to a Leader of Engine Development and collaborate with both game and central technology engineers and researchers to bring ML models into the hands of our players by deploying them directly into EA's games.
   

  

**Responsibilities**
* Design, build, and maintain robust end-to-end solutions for running machine learning models efficiently on a variety of devices.
* Partner with ML experts across EA to help adopt and scale new models and architectures optimized for on-device performance.
* Integrate ML solutions into our proprietary tools and game runtime environments on consoles, PCs, and mobile devices.
* Write clean, well-documented, and well-tested code that integrates smoothly with existing systems.
* Keep up to date with the latest advancements in Deep Learning, Reinforcement Learning, Generative AI, and related fields, through continuous learning and by attending internal and external conferences.
* Be an enthusiastic contributor to Frostbite's and EA's ML/AI communities - sharing your knowledge, collaborating with partners, and mentoring teammates along the way.


**Qualifications**
* 7+ years of hands-on software engineering experience with C++, including expertise in multithreading and low-level/near-hardware optimizations.
* Good knowledge of GPU programming.
* Proficient in debugging, profiling, and optimizing real-time software.
* Solid understanding and practice of software engineering fundamentals, such as version control, code reviews, documentation, automated testing, coding standards, CI/CD, issue tracking, and Agile.
* BSc or MSc degree in Computer Science, Engineering, Mathematics, or equivalent professional experience.
* Experience collaborating and sharing updates with developers and partners, including remote and asynchronous teams across different time zones.


**Nice To Have**
* Experience with implementing software tools to optimize code.
* Knowledge of ML frameworks such as PyTorch or TensorFlow.
* Knowledge of the ONNX format.
* Experience building, debugging, and shipping end-to-end ML systems in real-world production environments.


**About Electronic Arts**
 We’re proud to have an extensive portfolio of games and experiences, locations around the world, and opportunities across EA. We value adaptability, resilience, creativity, and curiosity. From leadership that brings out your potential, to creating space for learning and experimenting, we empower you to do great work and pursue opportunities for growth.
   

  

 We adopt a holistic approach to our benefits programs, emphasizing physical, emotional, financial, career, and community wellness to support a balanced life. Our packages are tailored to meet local needs and may include healthcare coverage, mental well-being support, retirement savings, paid time off, family leaves, complimentary games, and more. We nurture environments where our teams can always bring their best to what they do.
   

  

 Electronic Arts is an equal opportunity employer. All employment decisions are made without regard to race, color, national origin, ancestry, sex, gender, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, age, genetic information, religion, disability, medical condition, pregnancy, marital status, family status, veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by law. We will also consider employment qualified applicants with criminal records in accordance with applicable law. EA also makes workplace accommodations for qualified individuals with disabilities as required by applicable law.

title: Cloud Tech Lead - The lovely blend between People, Tech and Strategy
description:
Playground Tech
   

  

* Stockholm
* Hybrid


 Cloud Tech Lead - The lovely blend between People, Tech and Strategy
   

  

 Finding the right Tech Lead for a consultancy is a bit like finding a unicorn that actually knows how to code. You need the technical chops to build the thing, the patience to explain it to a Director, and the social grace to keep the team rowing in the same direction.
   

  

 At some companies, "Tech Lead" is just a fancy title for someone who spends 40 hours a week in spreadsheets. That’s not us. We’re looking for a Cloud Tech Lead who still gets a kick out of opening an IDE.
   

  

 You’re the bridge between high-level strategy and the actual "how-to." You aren't just managing the team; you are
 *in* 
 the team.
   

  

**The 75/25 Split (Give or Take)**
 We believe the best leaders are those who stay sharp. You can expect a minimum of 50% hands-on engineering work.
   

  

* The Easy Days: You’re building, deploying, and mentoring.
* The "Consultancy" Days: Some clients need a bit more hand-holding. When the going gets tough, you might shift to a 50/50 split, balancing deep-dive engineering with stakeholder coordination and expectation management.


 What you'll do
   

  

* Driving the Ship: This isn't an administrative role. You are a Team Lead in every sense; guiding project delivery from concept to "it's alive!"
* Translating "Tech" to "Human": You’ll be chatting with Directors and Engineering Managers one hour, and pair-programming with a dev the next. You need to speak both languages fluently
* Managing the Vibe: You know how to manage people and expectations in a clear, friendly and inclusve manner. We value autonomy, proactivity, and, most importantly, being someone people actually enjoy working with


 Who are you?
   

  

 Tech has probably been a vital form of interest in your life, and you might have started out tinkering with games, or solving problems by creating technical solutions, or maybe just had that mind-set sayin "I want to to know how and why it works!".
   

  

 You might have started of your career in sw development, system engineering or as DevOps and somewhere along the way you also learned that you have an interest in leading/mentoring people.
   

  

**You Would Probably Describe Yourself As**
**Technically Senior:** 
 You’ve been around the cloud block. You have the seniority to back up your decisions and the competence to earn the team's respect
   

  

**Project-Driven:** 
 You’ve got a track record of driving projects across the finish line, not just riding in the passenger seat
   

  

**A Proactive Type:** 
 You don’t wait for a ticket to tell you something is broken. You see a gap, you fill it
   

  

**Tech Skills**
* Architectural-level competence required in at least one of the three hyperscalers: AWS, Azure, or Google Cloud Platform
* Infrastructure as Code
* CI/CD experience with automated code deployment
* Container orchestration
* Observability tools
* Configuration management


**Bonus**
 Development experience in languages like Go or Python is beneficial for understanding developer experience, automation, and infrastructure as code
   

  

**Buzzword Bingo**
 AWS, Azure, Google Cloud Platform (GCP), Terraform, Pulumi, SDK, ArgoCD, GitHub Actions, Kubernetes, Grafana, Datadog, CloudWatch, Ansible, Go, Python, Docker
   

  

 Language requirement
   

  

 Fluency in Swedish is prioritized and English is a must. Why? Because
 *most* 
 of our collaborations and client projects are conducted in Swedish
   

  

 Let's Play!
   

  

 Applying to us is a breeze!
   

  

* Upload your resumé or use your Linkedin profile
* Answer the choice-questions (no "please-rewrite-your-resume" nonsense, promise)
* Feel free to include links to your portfolio, GitHub or similar if you have stuff you're proud of that you want to share with us


**Note**
* Playground Group does not provide visa sponsorship or relocation support.Applicants must already have a permanent and independent right to work in Sweden.
* Background check is a part of every recruitment process


 Welcome to Playground!
   

  

 At Playground, you work on real, complex assignments for well-established and ambitious clients, not bench projects or short-lived experiments. Our teams are trusted with production systems, platforms, and data that actually matter. Here growth comes from ownership, curiosity, and doing the work properly. You’re trusted to think, contribute, and keep getting better, one meaningful step at a time.
   

  

 Playground brings together specialized domains under one umbrella, with a shared culture and way of working, and easy access to broad technical expertise when it helps move the work forward.
   

  

 Buzz: Clound Engineer, Cloud Architect, PO, SRE, Site Reliability Engineer, Team Lead, Consultant, Konsult, Data Engineer, Systemutvecklare, SW Engineer, Backend Developer, DevOps, Molntjänst, Sverige, Stockholm
   

  

 Department Playground Tech Role Tech Lead, Cloud Locations Stockholm Remote status Hybrid
   

  

 Current Job Openings
   

  

* Cloud Developer - Is DevEx your "product" of choice? Playground Tech
* Stockholm
* Hybrid
* Cloud Architect who loves mentoring and leading? Come and play! Playground Tech
* Stockholm
* Hybrid


 More jobs
   

  

 Stockholm
   

  

**About Playground Group**
 At Playground Group, we start, own, and grow companies — and the people who lead them. We’re a corporate group with a sharp focus on technology and leadership, built around specialized companies that together form a uniquely strong offering.
   

  

 Founded in 2008
   

  

 Co-workers 80+
   

  

 Turnover +300M

title: Senior Software Engineer (Golang)
description:
**WHO WE ARE** 





 At Trustly, we're building a smarter, faster, and more secure financial future by revolutionizing the world of payments. As a global leader in Open Banking Payments, we are establishing Pay by Bank as the new standard at checkout, providing unparalleled freedom, speed, and ease to millions of consumers and merchants worldwide.
 




  






 Our Ambition: To build the world’s most disruptive payment network and redefine what the payment experience should feel like.
 




  






 Trustly is a global team of innovators, collaborators, and doers.  If you are driven by a strong sense of purpose and thrive in a dynamic, entrepreneurial, and high-growth environment, join us and be part of a team that’s transforming the way the world pays.
 




  




 ABOUT THE TEAM
   


 At Trustly, we aren’t just processing payments; we are building the world’s most resilient financial network. The Transfer & Funds team is the engine room of this mission. We are the architects and guardians of Trustly’s financial integrity, responsible for the core systems that ensure instant, reliable settlements for millions of users worldwide.
 


  


  


 We are currently undertaking a massive evolution of our infrastructure: transitioning our core financial logic from a high-volume Postgres monolith into a clean, Go-based distributed architecture. We are looking for a Senior Engineer who thrives on technical complexity and wants to leave a legacy by building our next-generation transactional systems.
 


  

 Why Join Transfer & Funds?
   


 This is a "change the engines while the plane is flying" opportunity. You will work on the most critical systems at Trustly, solving the industry's hardest problems in transactional consistency and scale. If you enjoy deep technical puzzles and want to own the bedrock of a global fintech leader, we want to hear from you.
 


  


 Apply now and help us architect the future of global payments.
 


  






  




**WHAT YOU'LL DO** 
  

  
* **Modernize the Core:** 
 Play a lead role in moving mission-critical logic out of a centralized Postgres monolith and into a high-velocity, domain-driven environment
  
* **Build for 99.99%:** 
 Architect and maintain the systems responsible for our
 **Transfer System and Transactional Ledger** 
 , ensuring absolute data integrity and high availability
  
* **Solve Distributed Complexity:** 
 Design services that handle high-concurrency and global scale, ensuring that every transaction is atomic, consistent, and resilient to failure
  
* **Define Target Architecture:** 
 Contribute to the long-term technical vision of how Trustly handles money movement, balancing the need for rapid feature delivery with the rigor of financial accounting
  

  


  

  

**The Tech Stack** 
  

  
* **Primary:** 
 Golang, PostgreSQL.
  
* **Architecture:** 
 Distributed Microservices, Event-Driven Patterns.
  
* **Infrastructure:** 
 AWS, EKS, Terraform, Datadog.
  

  


  

  


  




**WHO YOU ARE** 
  

  
* **Strong Go Expertise:** 
 You have
 **5+ years** 
 of experience building robust, production-grade backend systems in
 **Golang** 
 . You write idiomatic code and have a deep understanding of Go’s concurrency model and performance characteristics.
  
* **System Design & Distributed Architecture:** 
 You understand the nuances of distributed systems. You know how to build application-level safety nets, handle partial failures, and ensure "exactly-once" processing across service boundaries.
  
* **Relational Power-User:** 
 You have experience in SQL and relational databases, like
 **PostgreSQL** 
 . You understand transaction isolation, locking strategies, and how to refactor schemas under heavy production load.
  
* **Migration Experience:** 
 You’ve successfully moved logic out of legacy environments into modern architectures. You know how to bridge the old and the new without disrupting live operations.
  
* **High-Availability Focus:** 
 You design for failure. You value observability and have experience running mission-critical services in
 **AWS/Kubernetes** 
 environments.
  

  


  





  




  


**Our Fantastic Benefits**

 (varies by location)
 




 🌴
 **20 to 30 days of holiday** 
 to support a healthy work-life balance
 



 🥳
 **Monthly team outing allowance** 
 to enjoy social events with your colleagues
 



 👶
 **Parental leave top-up** 
 additional support for new parents
 



 🥐
 **Daily breakfast and on-site perks** 
 to make your workday smoother
 



 💚
 **Well being support** 
 our health allowance covers gym memberships, massages, and much more to help you feel your best
 



**PLUS** 
 additional benefits designed to enhance your work-life experience!
 



  





**Shape Your Role and Make an Impact**




 Join a dynamic environment where you can take ownership, drive change, and continuously embrace new challenges. We’re looking for dedicated and highly motivated individuals who thrive in a fast-paced environment and enjoy collaborating across different areas of the organisation.
 



 If your skills and experience align with this role, we’d love to hear from you!

title: Tech Lead - Data Engineering
description:
Playground Data
   

  

* Stockholm
* Hybrid


 Tech Lead - Data Engineering
   

  

 We're not looking for a manager who used to code. We're looking for a Data Engineer who also knows how to lead. You need the technical depth to architect a platform, the patience to explain a data model to a Director, and the social grace to keep the team aligned when the requirements change, again.
   

  

 At some companies, "Tech Lead" is just a fancy title for someone who spends 40 hours a week in meetings. That's not us. We're looking for a Data Tech Lead who still gets a kick out of getting their hands dirty in the data.
   

  

 You're the bridge between high-level strategy and the actual "how-to." You aren't just guiding the team; you are
 *in* 
 the team.
   

  

**The 75/25 Split (Give or Take)** 
 We believe the best leaders are those who stay sharp. You can expect a minimum of 50% hands-on engineering work.
   

  

* The Easy Days: You're building pipelines, modelling data, and mentoring.
* The "Consultancy" Days: Some clients need a bit more hand-holding. When the going gets tough, you might shift to a 50/50 split, balancing deep-dive engineering with stakeholder coordination and expectation management.


**What You'll Do**
**Drive the Platform:** 
 This isn't an administrative role. You lead data platform delivery from concept to production. Greenfield builds, major transformations, the real stuff.
   

  

**Translate "Data" to "Business":** 
 You'll be talking to Directors and Data Owners one hour and reviewing a DBT model with a junior engineer the next. You need to speak both languages fluently.
   

  

**Own the Architecture:** 
 You take a clear position on how data should flow, scale, and be structured. You bring opinions on information modelling, data governance frameworks, and platform design, and you can back them up.
   

  

**Grow the People Around You:** 
 You actively mentor and develop the engineers around you. You share knowledge, give honest feedback, and make the people next to you better. This is a non-negotiable part of the role.
   

  

**Manage the Vibe:** 
 You know how to manage people and expectations in a clear, friendly, and inclusive way. We value autonomy, proactivity, and most importantly being someone people actually enjoy working with.
   

  

**Who are you?**
 Data has probably been a running theme in your career. Maybe you started in software development or analytics engineering, and somewhere along the way realized you had both the technical depth and the pull to lead.
   

  

**You Would Probably Describe Yourself As**
**Technically Senior:** 
 You've built and operated data platforms in production. You have the seniority to back up your decisions and the competence to earn the team's respect.
   

  

**Architecture-Minded:** 
 You think beyond the next sprint. You have views on data modelling approaches, platform design, and how to build things that hold up over time.
   

  

**Governance-Aware:** 
 You understand why data governance matters in practice, not just as a checkbox, but as the difference between a platform teams trust and one they work around.
   

  

**Project-Driven:** 
 You've got a track record of driving initiatives across the finish line, not just riding in the passenger seat.
   

  

**A People Multiplier:** 
 You don't just deliver, you bring others along for the ride. You naturally create space for people to learn, challenge their thinking, and develop their craft. The team is stronger because you're in it.
   

  

**Tech Skills**
* Architectural-level competence in at least one major cloud provider (AWS, GCP, or Azure)
* Hands-on experience with modern data platforms such as Databricks or Snowflake
* Data modelling and information modelling
* Data governance frameworks and practices
* Python and SQL fluent, not just functional


**Buzzword bingo:** 
 AWS, Azure, GCP, Databricks, Snowflake, DBT, Python, SQL, Terraform
   

  

**Language requirement**
 Fluency in Swedish and English are required. Most of our collaborations and client projects are conducted in Swedish, and several of our clients have formal requirements for full professional fluency. Swedish citizenship is required for this role.
   

  

**Let's play!**
 Applying to us is a breeze!
   

  

* Upload your resumé or use your LinkedIn profile
* Answer the choice-questions (no "please-rewrite-your-resume" nonsense, promise)
* Feel free to include links to your portfolio, GitHub, or similar if you have things you're proud of


**Note:**

You’re trusted to think, contribute, and keep getting better, one meaningful step at a time.

title: Senior Platform Engineer
description:
**About Us**
 Legora is redefining how legal work gets done. Not built for lawyers, built with them. We work alongside the world’s best legal teams, who expect excellence, precision, and speed, and we hold ourselves to the same bar.
   

  

 Our AI-native workspace lets legal professionals move faster, think more clearly, and operate with sharper precision. By analysing thousands of documents in minutes and powering end-to-end workflows, we cut through complexity, teams can focus on what matters: judgment, strategy, and outcomes.
   

  

 1,000+ customers across 50+ countries trust us, including Cleary Gottlieb, Goodwin, Linklaters, White & Case, Dentons, and Barclays. We’ve scaled to
 **$100M+ in ARR** 
 , with teams across Europe, North America and APAC, and continue to expand through acquisitions including Qura, Walter AI and Graceview.
   

  

 We partner with world-class performers: including Aaron Judge and the New York Yankees, Ludvig Åberg (and his caddie), and campaigns featuring Jude Law.
   

  

 Joining Legora means three things.
   

  

* We lean in: ownership over titles, outcomes over intentions.
* We fight for excellence: high standards, direct, ego-free feedback.
* We grow together: as a team and with our customers.


 Mission before ego. Everyone contributes. No one coasts.
   

  

 If you’re driven by impact, pace, and raising the bar. This is the place.
   

  

**The role**
 As a
 **Platform Engineer** 
 at Legora, you’ll join an enabling team focused on making life easier for developers. Your mission: improve reliability, boost performance, and build a rock-solid platform that scales with the company. You'll work hands-on across infrastructure and backend systems, helping teams ship faster, safer, and smarter.
   

  

**What You Will Be Doing**
* Build the core infrastructure and reliability abstractions that all our engineering teams use
* Sit with all teams and help them structure their SRE efforts: observability, alerting, fault tolerance and reliability
* Proactively monitor our infrastructure and develop it for hyperscale and reliability
* Contribute to incident response and post-incident learning
* Work with Pulumi on primarily Azure, but also AI deployments on AWS and GCP


**Who You Are**
* You have years of experience as a backend engineer but have solid infrastructure, observability, monitoring know-how
* Experience with Kubernetes
* Experience with Pulumi (preferred) or similar IaC tools like AWS CDK, Terraform
* But most importantly, you are someone who
+ “Gets stuff done” and understands building a $10bn company isn’t always glamorous and takes hard work and long hours
+ Proactively and independently solves the most burning problems
+ Strives in a fast-paced environment where the answers aren’t always clear and processes are few


**Legora is an Equal Opportunity Employer**
 At Legora, we believe great teams are built on diversity of thought and experience. We’re proud to be an equal opportunity employer and committed to creating an inclusive, high-performance culture where everyone can do their best work. We welcome people of all backgrounds and don’t discriminate based on race, color, religion, national origin, gender, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, age, disability, veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by law.

title: Senior Software Engineer - Environmental Services
description:
**Senior .NET Developer - Stockholm** 




 Stockholm · Permanent · Hybrid (2-3 days in office) · Swedish-speaking
 



  





**The company** 




 I'm working with a Stockholm company in the environmental services space - a genuine player in the circular economy, turning waste and residual products into reusable resources. It's work with real environmental and social value: every day the team makes a tangible difference for the planet alongside thousands of customers. If you want your development work to sit behind something that's good for the environment, this is the role.
 



  





**The role** 




 This is a senior developer role on a small, close-knit team where everyone has a voice and there's genuine freedom in what you build. You'll bring experience and judgement to a team that values autonomy, helping shape not just the code but how things get built - working across a modern .NET stack on the platforms that run the business.
 



  





 It's a small team, so this suits an experienced developer who's driven, curious and social - comfortable engaging directly with stakeholders and customers, and confident taking ownership rather than waiting to be directed. You'll also have:
 


* An experienced colleague as a sponsor/mentor, and a thorough introduction to the business and its services
* Exposure across different departments and experienced colleagues
* A manager with deep knowledge of the area, strong leadership, and many years with the company



  





**Technical environment** 




 .NET Core, with React or Vue on the front end. Azure DevOps, PostgreSQL/databases and version control with Git.
 



  





**What we're looking for** 



* 5+ years of development experience
* Strong .NET Core (most important), plus React or Vue
* Solid database knowledge, ideally PostgreSQL
* Confident with Git and version control
* Driven, curious and outgoing - happy to engage with stakeholders and customers, and to take real ownership in a small team
* Fluent Swedish and English



  





**Why join** 



* A fun, small team where everyone's voice counts and you get real freedom and ownership in what you develop
* Work that genuinely matters - behind a greener, more circular economy
* A collaborative environment with experienced colleagues and strong support
* A collective agreement and a flexible hybrid setup



  





***Please apply using the Easy Apply function.***




  





***Application deadline is 03/07/2026, but applications will be processed on an ongoing basis so please apply ASAP if you are interested.***




  





***Any questions, please email t.collard-gentle@tenthrevolution.com***

title: Engineering Manager
description:
**About Us**
 Legora is redefining how legal work gets done. Not built for lawyers, built with them. We work alongside the world’s best legal teams, who expect excellence, precision, and speed, and we hold ourselves to the same bar.
   

  

 Our AI-native workspace lets legal professionals move faster, think more clearly, and operate with sharper precision. By analysing thousands of documents in minutes and powering end-to-end workflows, we cut through complexity, teams can focus on what matters: judgment, strategy, and outcomes.
   

  

 1,000+ customers across 50+ countries trust us, including Cleary Gottlieb, Goodwin, Linklaters, White & Case, Dentons, and Barclays. We’ve scaled to
 **$100M+ in ARR** 
 , with teams across Europe, North America and APAC, and continue to expand through acquisitions including Qura, Walter AI and Graceview.
   

  

 We partner with world-class performers: including Aaron Judge and the New York Yankees, Ludvig Åberg (and his caddie), and campaigns featuring Jude Law.
   

  

 Joining Legora means three things.
   

  

* We lean in: ownership over titles, outcomes over intentions.
* We fight for excellence: high standards, direct, ego-free feedback.
* We grow together: as a team and with our customers.


 Mission before ego. Everyone contributes. No one coasts.
   

  

 If you’re driven by impact, pace, and raising the bar. This is the place.
   

  

**The role**
 As an
 **Engineering Manager** 
 at Legora, you’ll lead by example—hands-on in the code while guiding a talented team of engineers. You’ll architect and deliver features that make legal professionals’ lives easier, drive technical excellence, and help shape a product used by forward-thinking law firms around the world. This is a role for someone who loves to build, mentor, and scale both people and systems.
   

  

**What You Will Be Doing**
* Design and implement end-to-end features across our fullstack platform
* Contribute directly to the codebase while supporting the growth of your team
* Lead architecture discussions and technical decision-making
* Ensure high standards in code quality, security, and performance
* Collaborate closely with Product and Design to ship value quickly and reliably
* Review code, give feedback, and mentor engineers at all levels
* Champion best practices in development, DevOps, and continuous improvement
* Work with Node.js, React, Azure, AWS and Kubernetes
* Work every day to ensure your team is healthy and efficient


**Who You Are**
- A seasoned fullstack or backend engineer with 1-3 years of leadership experience

- Comfortable being hands-on while also setting technical direction

- Skilled in building scalable products and owning complex systems end-to-end

- Strong communicator who leads with empathy and clarity

- Passionate about mentoring others and helping teams grow

- Confident working across backend, frontend, and infrastructure

- Curious, pragmatic, and always looking for smarter ways to build and deliver

- But most importantly, you are someone who:

* “Gets stuff done” and understands building a successful company isn’t always glamorous and takes hard work and long hours
* Proactively and independently solves the most burning problems
* Strives in a fast-paced environment where the answers aren’t always clear and processes are few
* Candidates will go through the same interview process as software engineering candidates (including a coding task and a technical interview), with additional interview steps at the end designed for the engineering manager role.
* This is a Stockholm-based, 5-day in-office role, we believe building together in person drives better outcomes.


**Legora is an Equal Opportunity Employer**
 At Legora, we believe great teams are built on diversity of thought and experience. We’re proud to be an equal opportunity employer and committed to creating an inclusive, high-performance culture where everyone can do their best work. We welcome people of all backgrounds and don’t discriminate based on race, color, religion, national origin, gender, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, age, disability, veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by law.

title: Systemutvecklare
description:
**Välkommen till Custodia Consulting - Upptäck möjligheterna att växa med oss!**
 Vi söker nu drivna Systemutvecklare för spännande uppdrag hos våra kunder. Hos Custodia strävar vi efter att bygga konkurrenskraftiga och kompetenta team med stark integritet inom automotive, life science, försvarsindustri och IT.
   

  

 Vi söker dig som vill arbeta i tekniskt utmanande miljöer där du får vara med och utveckla robusta och moderna system tillsammans med erfarna team.
   

  

**Om tjänsten**
 Som Custodia-konsult arbetar du med utveckling, vidareutveckling och förvaltning av verksamhetskritiska system hos våra kunder. Uppdragen varierar beroende på verksamhet och teknisk miljö, men gemensamt är att du får arbeta nära både teknik och verksamhet för att skapa hållbara lösningar.
   

  

 Du kommer att arbeta i team där samarbete, initiativförmåga och teknisk nyfikenhet värderas högt. Rollen passar dig som gillar att ta ansvar, bidra med idéer och vara med och utveckla både system och arbetssätt.
   

  

 Vissa av våra uppdrag sker inom säkerhetsklassade verksamheter, vilket kan innebära krav på svenskt medborgarskap och godkänd säkerhetsprövning.
   

  

**Arbetsuppgifter**
* Utveckla och vidareutveckla system och applikationer inom backend och fullstack
* Arbeta med Kotlin, Java, Spring Boot och Spring Framework i moderna utvecklingsmiljöer
* Delta i design, implementation och förbättring av tekniska lösningar
* Arbeta med integrationer, databaser och API:er
* Bidra till förbättringsarbete inom utvecklingsprocesser, drift och automation
* Samarbeta nära utvecklare, arkitekter och verksamhet i agila team


 Du kommer även att vara en del utav Custodia teamet där du kommer att ha en direkt inverkan på besluttagandet, utformningen av företagskulturen samt etableringen av värdegrunden både internt och externt.
   

  

**Vi söker dig som**
* Har relevant högskoleutbildning inom systemutveckling, datateknik eller motsvarande
* Har minst 5 års erfarenhet av systemutveckling inom relevant område
* Har god erfarenhet av Kotlin (JVM) och/eller Java
* Har god erfarenhet av Spring Boot / Spring Framework
* Kan arbeta på plats
* Är flytande i svenska och engelska, både i tal och skrift
* Är självgående och trivs med att ta ansvar i teammiljöer


****Det är meriterande om du har****
* Erfarenhet av agilt arbetssätt
* Erfarenhet av DevOps, CI/CD, Ansible, Helm eller Hauler
* Erfarenhet av arbete i Linuxmiljö
* Erfarenhet av Typescript och React
* Erfarenhet av XML och XPath
* Erfarenhet av databaser och containerteknologier


****För att lyckas i rollen har du följande personliga egenskaper:****
* Problemlösande
* Kommunikativ
* Team-player
* Analytisk


**Om oss**
 Custodia Consulting är ett konsultföretag med stort fokus på prestation med avsikt att skapa mervärde. Vi erbjuder högkvalitativa tjänster inom Projektledning, teknik, Life Science och digitala lösningar. Vår vision är att bygga starka och konkurrenskraftiga team med människor från olika bakgrund, livs- och arbetslivserfarenhet. Genom att bygga våra team med våra värderingar tror vi starkt på att skapa värde för våra kunder, men också för våra anställda.
   

  

 Vi på Custodia främjar arbetsglädje och välmående. Vi kommer därför att ha kontinuerliga avstämningar och workshops, där vi kan vara öppna och transparenta, utbyta erfarenheter och diskutera idéer.
   

  

 Vi har ett stort kontaktnätverk samt täta samarbeten med större konsultbolag, vilket gör det enklare för oss att placera våra konsulter i rätt uppdrag.
   

  

 Läs mer om oss här.
   

  

****Våra kärnvärden:****
* Integritet - Vi tar ansvar och är ett dedikerat och pålitligt team.
* Kvalitet - Vi levererar fantastiska resultat utan kompromisser.
* Inkludering - Alla är viktiga, vi skapar mångfaldiga och högpresterande team som kan skapa värde för våra kunder.


****Karriärsmöjligheter:****
 Vår passion för tillväxt och utveckling sträcker sig inte bara till våra kunder, utan även till våra medarbetare. Som ett växande konsultbolag erbjuder vi spännande karriärmöjligheter där du kan växa och blomstra tillsammans med oss. Vi tror på att investera i våra talanger och skapa en miljö där du kan utvecklas professionellt och personligt.
   

  

 Samtliga konsulter på Custodia har även en affärsmässig roll för att bidra till bolagsetableringen och tillväxten. Vi tillsammans kommer att arbeta för att sätta den önskvärda kulturen och se till att bolagets kärnvärden uppmärksammas hos våra kunder, partners och leverantörer.
   

  

**Vår rekryteringsprocess**
 Vi tillämpar löpande urval. Första steget är en telefonintervju, där vi går igenom din bakgrund och motivation. Därefter följer en djupintervju, där vi utforskar din kompetens och erfarenhet mer ingående.
   

  

 Vi strävar efter en objektiv och rättvis rekryteringsprocess och arbetar aktivt för att motverka diskriminering i enlighet med diskrimineringsgrunderna. Vår målsättning är att rekrytera på ett inkluderande och kompetensbaserat sätt, där alla kandidater bedöms utifrån sina meriter.
   

  

**Gå med oss på Custodia och skapa en framgångsrik karriär!**
 Läs mer på våran hemsida.

title: Back End-utvecklare
description:
**Backendutvecklare till Memlist AB** 




  





 Memlist AB utvecklar ett modernt, webbaserat medlemssystem som hjälper föreningar, organisationer och företag att administrera, interagera och kommunicera med sina medlemmar på ett effektivt sätt. Vår plattform används för bland annat medlemshantering, betalningar, medlemsförnyelser, kommunikation via e-post/SMS och verksamhetsstöd för organisationer med olika behov.
 



 Nu söker vi en erfaren backendutvecklare som vill vara med och vidareutveckla Memlists plattform, integrationer och datadrivna funktioner.
 



  





 Några av våra system finns att läsa om på https://marketeer.se, https://medlemskortet.se och https://memlist.se
   





**Om rollen** 




 Som backendutvecklare hos Memlist arbetar du med utveckling, vidareutveckling och förvaltning av våra backendtjänster. Du kommer att arbeta nära övriga utvecklare, produktägare och verksamhet för att skapa stabila, säkra och skalbara lösningar för våra kunder.
 



 Rollen passar dig som trivs med affärskritiska system, komplex datamodellering och praktisk problemlösning i en produktmiljö där kodkvalitet, säkerhet och långsiktig förvaltning är viktigt.
   





**Arbetsrollen** 




 Du kommer bland annat att:
 


* utveckla och underhålla backendfunktioner i Node.js med TypeScript
* arbeta med databaser, SQL-frågor, datamodeller och prestandaoptimering
* vidareutveckla integrationer mot externa system och tjänster
* bygga robusta API:er och interna tjänster
* bidra till tekniska vägval, kodgranskning och förbättring av utvecklingsprocesser
* säkerställa att lösningar är driftsäkra, testbara och skalbara
* arbeta med system som hanterar känslig information, persondata och affärskritiska flöden
* kontakt med kund och supportansvar



  

  





**Skallkrav** 




 För att vara aktuell för rollen behöver du ha dokumenterad erfarenhet av:
 


* backendutveckling med Node.js
* utveckling i TypeScript
* SQL-utveckling mot MariaDB
* SQL-utveckling mot PostgreSQL
* utveckling av produktionssatta system
* arbete med relationsdatabaser, datamodellering och optimering av SQL-frågor
* bekväm med att kommunicera med kunder via telefon, e-post och chatt på Svenska



**Du ska även ha:** 



* avlagd universitets- eller högskoleexamen inom datavetenskap, systemvetenskap, mjukvaruutveckling eller annan relevant IT-inriktning



**Meriterande** 




 Det är meriterande om du även har erfarenhet av:
 


* REST API:er och API-design
* databasmigreringar och versionshantering av databasscheman
* testning av backendkod, exempelvis enhets- och integrationstester
* Docker eller annan containerbaserad utvecklingsmiljö
* CI/CD och automatiserade deploymentflöden
* säker utveckling, GDPR och hantering av personuppgifter
* prestandaoptimering i större databaser
* integrationsutveckling mot betaltjänster, ekonomisystem eller kommunikationsplattformar
* arbete i SaaS- eller produktbolag
* Git, kodgranskning och agila arbetssätt



  





**Vem vi söker** 




 Vi söker dig som är strukturerad, ansvarstagande och noggrann. Du är trygg i backendutveckling och har god förståelse för hur tekniska beslut påverkar stabilitet, säkerhet och användarupplevelse över tid.
 



 Du tycker om att förstå verksamhetsproblem på djupet och omsätta dem till välfungerande tekniska lösningar. Du är lyhörd och kommer med förslag på nya idéer och produkter att lansera. Du är självgående men uppskattar samtidigt samarbete, kodgranskning och kunskapsdelning med kollegor.
 



 Du gillar att prata med kunderna och kunna hjälpa till med deras utmaningar.
 



  





**Vad vi erbjuder** 




 Hos Memlist får du arbeta med en produkt som skapar konkret nytta för organisationer, föreningar och deras medlemmar. Du blir en viktig del av ett utvecklingsteam där teknik, produkt och kundnytta hänger nära samman.
 



 Vi erbjuder en roll med stort tekniskt ansvar, möjlighet att påverka arkitektur och arbetssätt samt en miljö där långsiktig produktutveckling står i centrum.
 



 Vi arbetar heltid i vårt kontor i Gamla Stan.
 



  





**Din ansökan** 




 Skicka din ansökan med CV och en kort beskrivning av din dokumenterade erfarenhet av Node.js, TypeScript, MariaDB och PostgreSQL.
 



 Vi kommer att eventuellt hålla ett prov på plats i våra lokaler för att säkerställa kunskapen inom SQL.

title: Senior .NET utvecklare
description:
**Att jobba som Senior .NET utvecklare**
 Vi söker en senior .NET utvecklare som vill bli en del av ett team som bygger och utvecklar affärskritiska system inom försäkringsområdet. Du kommer att arbeta nära mjukvaruarkitekter, produktägare, testare och andra utvecklare för att designa, implementera och vidareutveckla högkvalitativa lösningar som är säkra, skalbara och långsiktigt hållbara.
   

  

 Tillsammans med teamet deltar du i arbete kring krav, design och systemarkitektur och bidrar till välgrundade tekniska beslut. Rollen har ett tydligt fokus på backendutveckling i .NET/C#, samtidigt som du även kommer att arbeta med och vidareutveckla interna frontendlösningar i React.
   

  

 Utvecklingsarbetet sker i en eventdriven arkitektur inom en säkerhetskritisk miljö med stora datamängder och höga krav på tillförlitlighet, prestanda och stabilitet. Du arbetar med integrationer mot andra system och deltar aktivt i det kontinuerliga arbetet med att förbättra kodkvalitet, tekniska lösningar och teamets arbetssätt.
   

  

**För att lyckas i rollen**
 Vi söker dig som trivs i en utvecklarroll där kvalitet, säkerhet och driftsstabila system står i fokus. Du har erfarenhet av att arbeta med affärskritiska applikationer och känner dig trygg i att ta ägandeskap för tekniska lösningar genom hela utvecklingskedjan – från idé och design till implementation, vidareutveckling och optimering.
   

  

 Du har ett strukturerat och analytiskt arbetssätt och uppskattar att samarbeta nära andra utvecklare i ett teamorienterat arbetssätt. Samtidigt delar du gärna med dig av din kompetens och bidrar aktivt till att utveckla både teamets arbetssätt och de tekniska plattformarna.
   

  

 Vi tror också att du är nyfiken på ny teknik och intresserad av att ligga i framkant, exempelvis inom AI-stödd systemutveckling, och ser värdet i verktyg som GitHub Copilot för att skapa ännu bättre lösningar.
   

  

**Vi Söker Dig Som Har Erfarenhet Av**
* Systemutveckling i C# och .NET
* Backendutveckling och systemintegrationer
* Eventdriven arkitektur eller meddelandebaserade lösningar, gärna NServiceBus eller RabbitMQ
* Utveckling av RESTful API:er
* Relationsdatabaser, gärna SQL Server, samt ORM-verktyg som EF Core
* Git och moderna CI/CD-flöden


**Vad händer nu?**
 Vi jobbar med löpande urval så tveka inte att skicka in din ansökan redan idag. I ansökan kommer vi att ställa ett antal urvalsfrågor som ligger till grund för första urvalet.
   

  

 Läs mer om hela vår rekryteringsprocess här.
   

  

 Om du har frågor kring rollen, vänligen kontakta rekryterande chef Anders Nivstrand på anders.nivstrand@skandia.se
   

  

 Om du har frågor kopplat till rekryteringsprocess, vänligen ta kontakt med Nina Fazeli på nina.fazeli@skandia.se
   

  

 Vi kommer att återkoppla till alla som söker oavsett om du går vidare eller inte.
   

  

 Vi ser fram emot din ansökan!
   

  

**Mer om IT**
 Vi på Skandia IT är stolta över att vara en kundorienterad enhet som levererar effektiva och säkra lösningar för våra kunder. Med cirka 600 engagerade medarbetare är vi mitt i en spännande transformationsresa, där vi utvecklas tillsammans och har kul på vägen. Framåt tillsammans är vårt motto!
   

  

 Vi arbetar agilt och har sedan 2017 använt oss av SAFe (Scaled Agile Framework) ramverket, där leverans och kundvärde alltid står i centrum. Våra tågledningar och team, jobbar verksamhetsbaserat med aktiviteter som sprintplanering, daily stand-ups, team-synk, code reviews och retrospektiv.
   

  

 Var 12:e vecka samlas vi alla i vår ljusa och inspirerande Ljusgård på kontoret i Stockholm för 2 planeringsdagar. Planeringsperioden består av 6 tvåveckorssprintar, där den sista sprinten är en innovationssprint. Här får du chansen att, själv eller tillsammans med ditt team, utveckla och förfina våra system.

title: RPG Utvecklare till Handelsbanken Stockholm
description:
***Bli RPG‑utvecklare på Handelsbanken och arbeta affärsnära med bankens centrala system***
*Vill du arbeta affärsnära med bankens centrala system och bidra till både stabil förvaltning och modernisering? Vi söker en RPG‑utvecklare som vill utvecklas i en miljö präglad av samarbete, stabilitet och långsiktighet.*
**Ditt team**
 Du tillhör ett område som ansvarar för centrala delar av bankens systemstöd. Ditt närmsta team består av utvecklare med olika bakgrund och erfarenhet, där vi tillsammans arbetar med att utveckla och förvalta affärskritiska system.
   

  

 Vi har en stark sammanhållning och en kultur som präglas av samarbete, kunskapsdelning och ett gemensamt ansvar för leveransen. Här är det naturligt att hjälpa varandra och bidra till teamets utveckling.
   

  

 Arbetet sker i en internationell kontext med nära samarbete med verksamheten, vilket ger dig möjlighet att arbeta affärsnära och få en god förståelse för hur våra lösningar används i praktiken. Vi arbetar agilt och är en del av ett större sammanhang där flera team tillsammans driver utveckling och förbättring av bankens systemstöd.
   

  

**Din roll**
 I rollen som RPG‑utvecklare arbetar du med förvaltning och vidareutveckling av bankens centrala system. Du är en del av flera initiativ kopplade till både befintliga lösningar och modernisering av plattform och betalningslösningar.
   

  

 Du arbetar nära verksamheten och har kontakt med kollegor och beslutsfattare i olika länder, vilket ger dig möjlighet att påverka lösningar som är viktiga för bankens kunder och affär.
   

  

 Dina arbetsuppgifter innefattar bland annat att:
   

  

* arbeta med utveckling och felsökning i IBMi‑miljö
* analysera och åtgärda problem samt genomföra orsaksanalyser
* bidra i moderniseringsinitiativ och vidareutveckling av system och lösningar
* säkerställa att lösningar uppfyller regulatoriska krav
* samarbeta med kollegor och verksamhet i internationella team
* bidra till förbättring av test- och utvecklingsprocesser


 Du arbetar både med egenutvecklade system och inköpta lösningar.
   

  

**Din profil**
 Vi söker dig som har erfarenhet av RPG‑utveckling och som trivs i en roll där samarbete är en naturlig del av arbetet. Du har ett lyhört och hänsynstagande sätt och bidrar till att skapa goda relationer, både inom teamet och i samarbetet med verksamheten. Samtidigt arbetar du strukturerat och tar ansvar för att planera och genomföra dina uppgifter med kvalitet och noggrannhet. Du har också ett lösningsorienterat förhållningssätt och tar dig an problem med ambitionen att förstå och hitta hållbara lösningar.
   

  

**För att lyckas i rollen ser vi gärna att du har:**
* Erfarenhet av RPG (ILE RPG, RPG IV/free format)
* Erfarenhet av IBMi och arbete med felsökning
* Kunskap inom DB2 (SQL/DDS) och CL
* Goda kunskaper i svenska och engelska


**Det är även meriterande om du har erfarenhet av:**
* Erfarenhet från bank eller betalningslösningar
* Erfarenhet av agilt arbetssätt
* Erfarenhet av test och kvalitetssäkring


**Välkommen med din ansökan!**
*Som ett steg i Handelsbankens rekryteringsprocess krävs en godkänd bakgrundskontroll på slutkandidat, utförd av en extern part, innan anställning kan ske.*
**Kontaktuppgifter**
**Rekryterande chef:** 
 Evelita Zakaityte, evelita.zakaityte@handelsbanken.se
   

  

**Talent Acquisition Partner:** 
 Julia Beard, julia.beard@handelsbanken.se
