title: Senior Corporate Infrastructure Engineer
description:
**About Wolt**
 At Wolt, we create technology that brings joy, simplicity and earnings to the neighborhoods of the world. In 2014 we started with delivery of restaurant food. Now we’re building the delivery of (almost) everything and you’ll find us in over 500 cities in 30 countries around the world. In 2022 we joined forces with DoorDash and together we keep on dreaming big and expanding across the globe.
   

  

 Working at Wolt isn’t always easy, but it’s definitely exciting. Here you’ll learn more, build more, and ship more than in most other companies. You’ll be challenged a lot, but also have a lot of fun on the way. So, if you’re a self-starter with drive and entrepreneurial spirit, this could be the ride of your life.
   

  

 We are looking for an experienced Corporate Infrastructure Engineer who will be focused on Wolt and Doordash´s IT Infrastructure. This role will build and scale our systems to support a fully automated infrastructure by leveraging various technologies. You will work closely with several teams here at DoorDash including (but not limited to): Security, IT, Network Engineering and Enterprise Systems.
   

  

 Our ideal candidate for this position will have the ability to identify problems and offer resolutions in a timely manner. You will be responsible for the design, development and implementation of corporate IT systems. You will report into the Manager, Infrastructure in our IT organization.
   

  

 What you’ll be doing
   

  

* Run and maintain our corporate infrastructure hosted in AWS
* Maintain and administer infrastructure deployment tools
* Be self motivated to build high quality, scalable systems with cross-functional teams
* Be able to think critically, get into the details and change directions rapidly
* Be able to handle multiple contending priorities and effectively plan your work accordingly
* Navigate existing technologies and quickly learn and adapt to new technologies
* Make recommendations for the improvement and development of IT systems


 Our humble expectations\*
   

  

* You have 4+ years of experience in an Infrastructure Engineering role
* You have demonstrated experience with Python, bash and other scripting fundamentals
* AWS, Azure, Kubernetes, Docker and GCP systems/tools of high familiarity
* You have 2+ years of experience with Infrastructure as a Code tools like Terraform, CloudFormation, Pulumi.
* You have 2+ years of experience working with microservices
* You understand that monitoring/observability is equally important. Experience with but not limited to tools like Prometheus, Grafana, Nagios, Datadog, Splunk.
* You have experience in configuration management and security management
* You are a self starter with attention to detail who understands the value in system monitoring


**Next steps**
 All applications are reviewed by a real human, so please be patient. Qualified candidates will have the opportunity to speak with a member of our recruiting team before moving on to talk with the hiring manager. This is a highly technical role so the next step will be a live scripting/coding interview, followed by chats with members of the team and cross-functional partners.
   

  

 We are looking to fill this role as quickly as possible, but are committed to finding the right candidate who will be successful in the role
   

  

 Our Commitment to Diversity and Inclusion
   

  

 We’re committed to growing and empowering a more inclusive community within our company, industry, and cities. That’s why we hire and cultivate diverse teams of people from all backgrounds, experiences, and perspectives. We believe that true innovation happens when everyone has room at the table and the tools, resources, and opportunity to excel.

title: EUC / Field Services Engineer
description:
**EUC / Field Services Engineer:** 




  





 For one of our globally leading tech clients we are urgently looking for an experienced EUC / Field Services Engineer for a long contract based in Stockholm, Sweden
 



  





 Its paying well and my client can interview asap!
 



  





  





**Key Responsibilities** 




 End-User & Desktop Support
 


* Provide onsite desktop, laptop, mobile, and tablet support.
* Troubleshoot hardware and software issues.
* Support Windows OS, Office 365, and enterprise applications.
* Handle OS version upgrades, profile recreations, and vulnerability remediation activities.
* Monitor support queues and manage ticket dispatching.



 Device Lifecycle Management
 


* Laptop provisioning and configuration for new starters.
* Asset management and inventory tracking.
* Coordinate hardware repairs, break-fix activities, and replacements.
* Manage loaner devices and recycle cages.
* Coordinate equipment ordering and returns with vendors.



 Workplace & Smart Hands Support
 


* Provide Smart Hands support for Networks, Servers, Storage, Printers, Conferencing, and Collaboration tools.
* Meeting room and event technology support.
* Desk setup, office moves, and workplace technology maintenance.
* Support crisis management activities when required.



 SCCM & Endpoint Management
 


* SCCM administration and troubleshooting.
* Mass OS deployments and imaging projects.
* Maintain multiple OS images and hardware models.
* Support PXE boot and DHCP configurations.
* Knowledge of Microsoft OEM Pre-installation Kit (OPK).
* Intune administration and endpoint management.



 User Engagement & Training
 


* Conduct IT induction sessions for new employees.
* Provide user training on new technologies.
* Manage local communications and announcements.
* Handle user escalations, complaints, and service follow-ups.
* Provide VIP support services.



  





**Essential Skills** 



* Field Services / Desktop Support experience.
* Windows 10 administration and troubleshooting.
* Microsoft Office 2016 / Office 365 support.
* Desktop Support Engineer or Technical Support Engineer experience.
* Remote troubleshooting expertise.
* Windows Imaging and OS Deployment experience.
* SCCM and Intune administration.
* Networking fundamentals:
* DNS (nslookup)
* Traceroute
* Ping
* Packet capture tools
* Smart Hands and collaboration technology support.
* Office move and workplace technology project experience.
* Strong coordination and stakeholder management skills.



  





 Key Technologies
 


* Windows 10
* Microsoft Office 365
* SCCM
* Microsoft Intune
* PXE
* DHCP
* Active Directory
* Mobile Device Support
* Hardware Lifecycle Management
* Endpoint Management
* Collaboration & Conferencing Systems



  





  





 If this is you, then please do not delay in applying!

title: Marketing Intern - Sweden (Part-Time, Fall Semester)
description:
**About Wolt**
 At Wolt, we create technology that brings joy, simplicity and earnings to the neighborhoods of the world. In 2014 we started with delivery of restaurant food. Now we’re building the delivery of (almost) everything and you’ll find us in over 500 cities in 30 countries around the world. In 2022 we joined forces with DoorDash and together we keep on dreaming big and expanding across the globe.
   

  

 Working at Wolt isn’t always easy, but it’s definitely exciting. Here you’ll learn more, build more, and ship more than in most other companies. You’ll be challenged a lot, but also have a lot of fun on the way. So, if you’re a self-starter with drive and entrepreneurial spirit, this could be the ride of your life.
   

  

**Location:** 
 Stockholm, Sweden
   

  

**Team:** 
 Local Marketing
   

  

**Reporting to:** 
 Marketing Specialist / Marketing Manager
   

  

**Duration:** 
 September–December 2026 (12 weeks)
   

  

**Hours:** 
 20 hours per week or as otherwise agreed
   

  

 At Wolt, we're building the leading local commerce platform across Europe and Asia, helping people discover and get what they need, quickly and reliably.
   

  

 Marketing plays a key role in how we connect with customers, support local merchants, and bring our brand to life across channels. From local campaigns and social media initiatives to content creation and influencer collaborations, we are constantly looking for new ways to engage audiences and create meaningful customer experiences.
   

  

 As part of Wolt's Fall Semester Internship Program, we're looking for an ambitious final-year university student to join our Marketing team and gain hands-on experience working on real marketing initiatives with visible business impact.
   

  

**About The Role**
 This is not a traditional internship focused on observation and administrative support.
   

  

 As a Marketing Intern, you will support the execution of commercial and content marketing initiatives at the local level. Working closely with Marketing Specialists and Managers, you'll help bring campaigns to life by coordinating and executing tasks, preparing content, and assisting with operational and creative execution across multiple channels.
   

  

 This role is ideal for a student interested in pursuing a career in marketing, communications, social media, or consumer technology.
   

  

**What You'll Be Doing**
**Campaign Execution & Content Coordination**
* Assist in creating, localizing, and publishing marketing content across multiple channels, including in-app banners
* Use internal tools to help set up campaigns, schedule content and upload marketing assets
* Support the localization of global and regional marketing materials for the Swedish market
* Track campaign setup tasks and checklists to ensure assets are ready and campaigns are delivered on time


**Creative & Content Production**
* Collaborate with designers and content creators by preparing briefs, managing asset versions, and coordinating feedback
* Help organize marketing assets and ensure content is accurate and ready for launch
* Collect and organize user-generated content (UGC) for use across social media and campaign channels


**Social Media & Influencer Marketing**
* Help schedule and publish content on local social media channels under the guidance of the Marketing Specialist or Manager
* Assist with influencer outreach and campaign coordination
* Organize product deliveries, collect campaign content and coordinate timelines for influencer collaborations


**Cross-Functional Coordination**
* Assist in uploading and maintaining materials related to local merchant campaigns
* Coordinate timelines between the marketing team and other internal stakeholders to support the smooth rollout of initiatives


**What We're Looking For**
**Must Have**
* Currently in the final year of a Bachelor's or Master's degree
* Strong interest in marketing, communications, social media, or consumer technology
* Excellent organizational and coordination skills
* Strong attention to detail and ability to manage multiple tasks simultaneously
* Strong communication skills in English
* Proactive attitude and willingness to learn and take ownership


**Nice to Have**
* Previous internship, student organization, or project experience in marketing, communications, business, or startups
* Experience with social media platforms and content creation
* Familiarity with project coordination and stakeholder management
* Interest in technology, e-commerce, marketplaces or consumer brands


**What Success Looks Like**
 By the end of the internship, you will have:
   

  

* Contributed to the successful delivery of multiple local marketing campaigns
* Supported content creation and campaign execution across various marketing channels
* Developed practical experience in campaign operations, social media and influencer marketing
* Built strong stakeholder management and project coordination skills
* Gained hands-on experience working in a fast-paced marketing environment


**Why this role matters**
 This internship is designed to give talented students meaningful responsibility while helping Wolt identify future marketing talent.
   

  

 You'll gain practical experience across campaign execution and measurement, social media, influencer and content marketing, as well as marketing operations while working on projects that directly impact how Wolt grows in Sweden.
   

  

 High-performing interns may be considered for future full-time opportunities following graduation.
   

  

**Why join Wolt?**
* Work on real projects with visible business impact
* Learn from experienced marketing professionals
* Gain hands-on experience across multiple areas of marketing
* Collaborate with teams across Marketing, Creative, Commercial, and Operations
* Build practical skills that accelerate your marketing career
* Create a pathway toward future full-time opportunities at Wolt


**Next steps**
 If you're excited about learning how modern marketing works, developing practical experience, and making an impact while you study, we'd love to hear from you.
   

  

 After submitting your application, our Talent Acquisition team will review your profile. If it looks like a good fit, you'll go through a short interview process with the hiring manager and team. The process is designed to be transparent, efficient, and give you a real sense of what working at Wolt is like.
   

  

 Please note that we do not accept applications sent by email, please submit your application through our careers website.
   

  

 Our Commitment to Diversity and Inclusion
   

  

 We’re committed to growing and empowering a more inclusive community within our company, industry, and cities. That’s why we hire and cultivate diverse teams of people from all backgrounds, experiences, and perspectives. We believe that true innovation happens when everyone has room at the table and the tools, resources, and opportunity to excel.

title: Director - Workplace Technology
description:
**About Us**
 Legora is redefining how legal work gets done. Not built for lawyers, built with them. We work alongside the world’s best legal teams, who expect excellence, precision, and speed, and we hold ourselves to the same bar.
   

  

 Our AI-native workspace lets legal professionals move faster, think more clearly, and operate with sharper precision. By analysing thousands of documents in minutes and powering end-to-end workflows, we cut through complexity, teams can focus on what matters: judgment, strategy, and outcomes.
   

  

 1,000+ customers across 50+ countries trust us, including Cleary Gottlieb, Goodwin, Linklaters, White & Case, Dentons, and Barclays. We’ve scaled to
 **$100M+ in ARR** 
 , with teams across Europe, North America and APAC, and continue to expand through acquisitions including Qura, Walter AI and Graceview.
   

  

 We partner with world-class performers: including Aaron Judge and the New York Yankees, Ludvig Åberg (and his caddie), and campaigns featuring Jude Law.
   

  

 Joining Legora means three things.
   

  

* We lean in: ownership over titles, outcomes over intentions.
* We fight for excellence: high standards, direct, ego-free feedback.
* We grow together: as a team and with our customers.


 Mission before ego. Everyone contributes. No one coasts.
   

  

 If you’re driven by impact, pace, and raising the bar. This is the place.
   

  

 The Role
   

  

 As our Director of Workplace Technology, you will architect and operationalise the AI-enabled infrastructure that powers Legora’s global scale. You will design and build the systems, automations, and processes that ensure we operate with precision, speed, and minimal overhead - creating an operational foundation that is robust, auditable, and built to scale seamlessly.
   

  

**Your mandate is simple:** 
 Build the most automated, AI-powered, operationally excellent organisation - empower our staff to do more with less.
   

  

 You will partner across Product, Engineering, GTM, and Legal to translate organisational complexity into elegant, scalable architectures and ensure that meaningful leverage comes from technology not people.
   

  

 What You’ll Do
   

  

 You will have direct ownership over how Legora operates and scales. The systems, automations, and processes you design will materially impact the business.
   

  

 In this role, you will operate as the end-to-end owner of our operational systems and AI-enabled workflows, responsible for:
   

  

* Leading complex, cross-functional operational initiatives across IT, Revenue Operations, Product Operations, Finance, and People
* Translating ambiguous business needs into structured requirements, implementation roadmaps, and measurable success metrics
* Designing, implementing, and owning AI-enabled systems and automations that replace manual workflows
* Building and maintaining the integrations that connect our operational stack end to end
* Identifying bottlenecks and eliminating them through tooling, automation, and smarter system design
* Establishing governance, documentation, and lifecycle standards to ensure durability and accountability
* Creating visibility through dashboards and KPI frameworks that drive disciplined decision-making
* Owning operational simplicity and leverage across the organisation


 You will define how a lean, AI-first company should operate - and ensure it actually happens.
   

  

 Who You Are
   

  

 You are an execution-focused systems builder with strong ownership and sound judgment. You don’t just improve processes - you take responsibility for making them work. You believe exceptional organisations stay lean by design - and you are ready to own the systems that make that possible.
   

  

**You Bring**
* 7–12+ years of experience in operations, systems management, solutions engineering, or process design in high-growth environments
* A proven track record of implementing software, building integrations, and automating workflows end to end
* Strong technical fluency and comfort designing scalable, event-driven architectures in the cloud
* Experience aligning technical and non-technical stakeholders around outcomes
* A structured, analytical mindset with a bias toward automation over coordination
* Comfort operating in dynamic environments where systems must be built, not inherited


 Why This Role Matters
   

  

 This is a foundational role. The systems and processes you build will determine how we scale - not by adding people, but by adding capability. You will help create an organisation where AI is embedded in every workflow, where operations run with speed and precision, and where a small, exceptional team can achieve what typically takes hundreds.
   

  

 If we succeed, it will be because we built one of the most automated, AI-native operational engines in the world - and you will have been the architect behind it.
   

  

**Legora is an Equal Opportunity Employer**
 At Legora, we believe great teams are built on diversity of thought and experience. We’re proud to be an equal opportunity employer and committed to creating an inclusive, high-performance culture where everyone can do their best work. We welcome people of all backgrounds and don’t discriminate based on race, color, religion, national origin, gender, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, age, disability, veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by law.

title: Solutions Architect
description:
**Technical Solution Architect - Stockholm** 




 Stockholm · Permanent · Hybrid (2-3 days in office) · Swedish-speaking
 



  





**The organisation** 




  





 We are working with a well-established Stockholm organisation whose work carries genuine societal impact - something the people there really believe in and that sets it apart from a typical commercial environment. The work is Sweden-focused but internationally connected, with close collaboration alongside equivalent bodies abroad.
 



 They're in the middle of a significant digitalisation drive, which makes it a strong time to join on the technology side - there's real momentum behind modernising how they build and deliver their platforms, and Sweden is something of a European frontrunner in this space.
 



  





**The role** 




 This is a senior Solution Architect position with a genuine holistic remit, working across both ongoing operations and new development. You'll start by getting to grips with the full landscape, then take ownership of coordinating and guiding architecture decisions across the organisation's digital initiatives.
 



  





 Day to day, you will:
 


* Oversee and align multiple parallel initiatives - including a new website build, a legacy site decommission, and an AI-powered API portal
* Ensure everything fits into a cohesive, MACH-oriented architecture built on microservices and cloud
* Work closely with the Tech Lead and a development team of around 15, providing strategic direction rather than writing the code yourself
* Drive architecture governance, including leading an architecture council
* Support the shift toward a 24/7, service-oriented platform business
* Contribute to decisions involving AI, APIs, Azure and modern microservices



  





 In short: a strategic architect role that balances big-picture ownership with hands-on support across delivery - high influence, with a real chance to shape your area and grow in it.
 



  





**Technical environment** 




 .NET, Azure Cloud, Azure DevOps, SQL Server and REST APIs, moving toward a MACH-oriented microservices setup.
 



  





**What we're looking for** 



* Several years in a technical/solution architect role - or at minimum a genuine 50/50 development-and-architecture role over several years
* Experience with, or a strong interest in, AI
* Experience with, or interest in, microservices architecture (beneficial rather than essential)
* Fluent Swedish



  





**Why join** 



* Work with real societal value, in an organisation whose output genuinely matters across Swedish industry and beyond
* High influence and ownership of your area, with genuine room to grow in the role
* A diverse and collaborative organisation
* Strong benefits - a collective agreement, the Benify portal, and additional holiday on top of the standard six weeks
* Flexible hybrid setup (2–3 days in the office)



  





***Please apply using the Easy Apply function.***




  





***Application deadline is 03/07/2026, but applications will be processed on an ongoing basis so please apply ASAP if you are interested.***




  





***Any questions, please email t.collard-gentle@tenthrevolution.com***

title: Informationsarkitekt
description:
**Jobbeskrivning**
 Vill du arbeta i gränslandet mellan verksamhet och teknik och skapa struktur i komplex information? På Sopra Steria söker vi nu en
 **Informationsarkitekt** 
 som vill bidra till att göra organisationers information begriplig, strukturerad och möjlig att använda fullt ut.
   

  

 Som informationsarkitekt hos oss arbetar du i varierande och ofta samhällsviktiga uppdrag där
 **information, data och begreppslig tydlighet är avgörande för framgång** 
 . Du hjälper våra kunder att skapa förståelse för sin information och lägga grunden för hållbara digitala lösningar.
   

  

 I rollen ansvarar du för att strukturera och definiera verksamhetens information så att den blir
 **begriplig för kunden och möjlig att realisera tekniskt** 
 . Du arbetar nära verksamheten, arkitekter och utvecklingsteam för att säkerställa att information förstås på samma sätt genom hela organisationen – från strategi till implementation.
   

  

 En central del av arbetet är
 **informations- och datamodellering** 
 , där du översätter verksamhetens behov till konceptuella och logiska modeller som stödjer både dagens och morgondagens lösningar.
   

  

**Dina arbetsuppgifter**
**Som Informationsarkitekt Kommer Du Bland Annat Att**
* Kartlägga och strukturera verksamhetens informationslandskap
* Ta fram begreppsmodeller, informationsmodeller och logiska datamodeller
* Etablera gemensamma begrepp och semantiska definitioner i organisationen
* Säkerställa spårbarhet mellan verksamhetsbehov, information och systemlösningar
* Bidra till arbetet med data governance, masterdata och datakvalitet
* Ta fram metoder, riktlinjer och arbetssätt för informationsarkitektur
* Stödja projekt och utvecklingsteam i frågor kring information och datamodellering


 Du arbetar nära verksamhetsrepresentanter, arkitekter och utvecklingsteam och bidrar till att skapa en
 **gemensam förståelse för information och dess betydelse i organisationen** 
 .
   

  

**Vem är du?**
 Du är en strukturerad och analytisk person som drivs av att skapa
 **ordning, tydlighet och kvalitet i information** 
 . Du trivs i dialog med verksamheten och har förmågan att omsätta komplexa behov till modeller och strukturer som går att realisera i teknik.
   

  

**Vi Tror Att Du**
* Har relevant utbildning inom IT, informationsarkitektur, systemvetenskap eller liknande
* Har god erfarenhet av informationsmodellering och/eller datamodellering i komplexa miljöer
* Är van att arbeta verksamhetsnära och facilitera workshops kring begrepp och information
* Har god förståelse för skillnaden mellan informationsmodell och datamodell
* Har erfarenhet av modelleringsspråk som UML och/eller ER-diagram
* Har arbetat i eller nära initiativ inom data management, MDM eller informationsstyrning


 Du har en god kommunikativ förmåga och kan förklara informations- och datamodeller på ett pedagogiskt sätt för både tekniska och icke-tekniska intressenter. Du behärskar
 **svenska och engelska flytande i tal och skrift** 
 .
   

  

**Varför välja Sopra Steria?**
* En plats där din kompetens verkligen gör skillnad – Vi bygger smarta och hållbara lösningar som förbättrar samhälle och verksamheter med användarupplevelsen i centrum.
* Stark företagskultur med Power of Sharing – Vi tror på kraften i kunskapsdelning, transparens och samarbete. Hos oss når vi längre tillsammans.
* Toppbetyg som arbetsplats – Great Place to Work har rankat oss år efter år som en av Sveriges bästa arbetsplatser och vi har mottagit utmärkelsen Karriärföretag flera år i rad.
* Del av en europeisk ledare inom digital transformation – Med över 50 000 experter i många länder kan vi både påverka stora projekt och ge dig möjligheter att växa globalt.
* Värderingar som genomsyrar allt vi gör – Vi sätter kundens behov i fokus, värderar respekt, professionalitet, nyfikenhet och en kollektiv förmåga att agera positivt och skapa hållbara resultat tillsammans.


**Ansökan**
 Nu vill vi gärna höra lite om dig! Ansök enkelt genom att antingen ladda upp ditt CV eller länka direkt till ditt LinkedIn-konto via annonsen. Om det fortfarande finns något du funderar över är du välkommen att kontakta rekryterande chef Marie Berg, marie.berg@soprasteria.com eller Talent Acquisition Partner Svante Larsson, svante.larsson2@soprasteria.com
   

  

 Vi ser fram emot att få höra ifrån dig!
   

  

 På Sopra Steria ställer vi höga krav på både kompetens och säkerhetsmedvetenhet hos våra medarbetare. Innan en eventuell anställning kommer du därför att genomgå en bakgrundskontroll, och vid intervju behöver du kunna uppvisa giltig legitimation.

title: Junior Information Security Officer
description:
**WHO WE ARE** 





 At Trustly, we're building a smarter, faster, and more secure financial future by revolutionizing the world of payments. As a global leader in Open Banking Payments, we are establishing Pay by Bank as the new standard at checkout, providing unparalleled freedom, speed, and ease to millions of consumers and merchants worldwide.
 




  






 Our Ambition: To build the world’s most disruptive payment network and redefine what the payment experience should feel like.
 




  






 Trustly is a global team of innovators, collaborators, and doers.  If you are driven by a strong sense of purpose and thrive in a dynamic, entrepreneurial, and high-growth environment, join us and be part of a team that’s transforming the way the world pays.
 




  




**ABOUT THE TEAM** 
  


 The Security team is Trustly's first line of defence. We do the hands-on security work - running risk assessments, reviewing vendors, maintaining policies and procedures, driving business continuity and disaster recovery, and making sure security is embedded in how Trustly builds and operates its products. We work closely with engineering, legal, finance, risk & compliance, HR and senior leadership, and partner with the second line for governance and oversight.
 


  

**ABOUT THE ROLE** 
  


 We are looking for a Junior Information Security Officer to join the Information Security team, reporting to the Director of Security in Stockholm. The role sits in the first line of defence, meaning you will be hands-on with security work - executing risk assessments, vendor reviews and compliance activities rather than overseeing them from a distance.
 


  


 This is a great role for someone early in their security career who wants to build broad experience across governance, risk management, compliance and operations in a regulated payments environment. You will support the team across its full scope of work, learn fast, contribute from day one, and grow into increasing independence over time.
 




  




**What you'll do** 
  

  
* Support the development and maintenance of Trustly's information security framework (ISMS), including drafting and updating instructions and routines
  
* Conduct information security risk assessments and help maintain the risk register
  
* Perform security assessments of third-party vendors and partners as part of onboarding and ongoing oversight
  
* Support business continuity, disaster recovery and crisis management activities, including planning and testing
  
* Assist in maintaining security controls across areas such as access management, internal fraud prevention, monitoring and segregation of duties
  
* Support compliance activities, including preparation for audits and certifications
  
* Respond to customer due diligence requests and security questionnaires
  
* Contribute to security awareness initiatives, training materials and internal communications
  
* Help track incidents, audit findings and remediation activities to ensure timely resolution
  

  


  


  




**Who you are** 
  

  
* 1 - 3 years of experience in information security, IT risk, compliance or a related field; Internship or graduate experience counts
  
* Foundational understanding of information security principles and familiarity with frameworks such as ISO/IEC 27001 or NIST CSF
  
* Strong written and verbal communication skills in English - you can write clearly and explain security concepts to a non-technical audience
  
* Analytical and structured in your approach to work, with attention to detail
  
* Curious and eager to learn - you want to understand how regulations, risk and technology connect in practice
  
* Comfortable working across teams and asking good questions
  
* Working towards or holding a relevant certification (CISM, ISO 27001 Lead Implementer, CISA, CISSP or similar) is a plus but not required
  
* Fluent in English, written and spoken; Swedish is a bonus but not a requirement
  

  


  





  




  


**Our Fantastic Benefits**

 (varies by location)
 




 🌴
 **20 to 30 days of holiday** 
 to support a healthy work-life balance
 



 🥳
 **Monthly team outing allowance** 
 to enjoy social events with your colleagues
 



 👶
 **Parental leave top-up** 
 additional support for new parents
 



 🥐
 **Daily breakfast and on-site perks** 
 to make your workday smoother
 



 💚
 **Well being support** 
 our health allowance covers gym memberships, massages, and much more to help you feel your best
 



**PLUS** 
 additional benefits designed to enhance your work-life experience!
 



  





**Shape Your Role and Make an Impact**




 Join a dynamic environment where you can take ownership, drive change, and continuously embrace new challenges. We’re looking for dedicated and highly motivated individuals who thrive in a fast-paced environment and enjoy collaborating across different areas of the organisation.
 



 If your skills and experience align with this role, we’d love to hear from you!
 **Apply now and submit your CV in English.** 



  


 We may use artificial intelligence (AI) tools to support parts of the hiring process, such as reviewing applications, analyzing resumes, or assessing responses and identifying potential inconsistencies or verification signals in application materials based on available information. These tools assist our recruitment team but do not replace human judgment. Final hiring decisions are ultimately made by humans. If you would like more information about how your data is processed, please contact us.

title: Site Reliability Engineer
description:
*Are you ready to redefine the financial industry? Do technical challenges make your heart beat faster? Then Nordnet is the place for you!*
**Shaping How Reliability Scales At Nordnet**
 As Nordnet has grown, so has our need for a dedicated reliability function. Our teams have built solid production systems, and we've reached the point where centralizing reliability expertise (observability, SLOs, resilience patterns) will take us to the next level.
   

  

 We're creating a new SRE stream within our Backend Platform team (BAP), and we're looking for someone who wants to define what that function looks like. You'll shape the tooling, practices, and standards that help our engineering org operate with more confidence at scale.
   

  

**What You Will Do**
 You'll work in a team with a dual mandate: maintaining and evolving our backend platform (root modules, standard libraries, Spring Boot frameworks) while building out a new SRE stream. The two are deeply connected. Our shared HTTP starter already has circuit breakers and bulkheads baked in, and observability instrumentation lives in the same libraries. Your main responsibilities will be:
   

  

* Own the observability stack: Take ownership of the full chain: metrics, logging, tracing, alerting, dashboards. Make it so developers get useful observability for free by using the standard stack, and extending it is straightforward.
* Extend the SLO framework: We have latency SLOs in place; availability is next. You'll mature our SLO practice into the shared language for reliability-vs-feature tradeoffs across the org.
* Run reliability reviews: Work proactively with product teams to find blind spots and resiliency gaps. You're an invited expert who helps, not an inspector who grades homework.
* Standardize for resilience: Build patterns like circuit breakers, retries, timeouts, bulkheads, and graceful degradation directly into the platform so the resilient path is the easy path.
* You start consultative: building trust, proving value, earning credibility with product teams. Over time, with organizational backing, this evolves into a more structured practice, including production readiness reviews, reliability standards, and error budget-driven conversations.


**Who You Are**
 You're a senior backend engineer who's deeply curious about how systems behave in production, not just how they're designed to behave.
   

  

* You've built and operated large-scale production systems and know what it takes to keep them running well.
* You think in platform terms. You'd rather solve a problem once for 30 teams than fix it once for one.
* You can sell, not just build. This role is as much about influencing teams who aren't used to external input on their production posture as it is about writing code. You build trust before you build tools.
* You care about developer experience. Reducing friction for your colleagues is a feature, not a side effect.
* You have strong experience with Java and Spring Boot. Familiarity with GCP, Infrastructure as Code (Terraform), and frontend technologies like React & TypeScript is a plus.


**Who are we?**
 Nordnet started with a passionate belief that it was possible to change the hierarchical financial world. We became a part of redefining the industry when we gave customers power over their own savings. You will become part of a journey that has only just begun toward the vision of making our customers the best investors they can be.
   

  

 We are 1000 employees in Stockholm, Oslo, Helsinki, Copenhagen and Frankfurt. We cooperate, exchange ideas, and use our core competence to constantly improve the way we meet our customers. Open communication, creativity, and engagement are important parts of our everyday life at the office.
   

  

 Our culture is based on passion, simplicity, and transparency. We have a flat organization where everyone can talk to everyone, and we believe in an environment where every effort counts and where everyone is seen.
   

  

**Application**
 Please note that we will start reviewing applications after the summer holidays. Do you have questions about the role? Contact our Senior Talent Acquisition Partner, Sophie Esphagen, at sophie.esphagen@nordnet.se.
   

  

 We want to inform you that Nordnet conducts mandatory credit and background checks, as well as drug testing as part of our recruitment process.
   

  

 We look forward to receiving your application!

title: Data Platform Engineer
description:
Bannerflow is a fast-growing global SaaS company building a Creative Intelligence Platform that enables enterprise brands to predict, create, and optimize advertising across channels.
   

  

 We’re on a company-wide AI transformation journey, where AI and agentic workflows are becoming a core part of how we build and innovate, with high expectations on adoption across all teams.
   

  

 Our teams are highly autonomous, collaborative, and we are now looking for another passionate engineer to join us!
   

  

**The challenge: Scaling data for the future**
 Our Platform Serves 500+ Million Impressions Daily, With Peaks Over 1 Billion Impressions, And We’re Just Getting Started. We’re Currently Rebuilding Our Entire Data Infrastructure From The Ground Up To Adopt a Data Lakehouse Approach, Enabling
   

  

* Scalability & Flexibility: Handling billions of data points while staying cost-efficient and future-proof.
* Data-Driven Features (AI & ML): Enabling predictive models and automation based on historical data.
* Business Intelligence & Analytics: Delivering insights directly to customers in our platform.


 To make this vision a reality, we’re looking for an experienced Data Platform Engineer to join our Platform team, and take full ownership of designing and building a scalable, high-performance data platform that will power the next generation of our product.
   

  

**Are you + Bannerflow = True? 🦋**
 We believe this is an opportunity for you who want to take the technical lead and define our data platform and best practices. Your work will directly impact or product vision, customers, and drive innovation in Ad Tech as part of an autonomous tech team. You'll tackle cutting-edge challenges in large-scale data processing, ML pipelines, and advanced analytics.
   

  

 At Bannerflow, we live by our values of passion, collaboration, and challenge. You'll be part of a vibrant culture with activities like board game nights, Friday beers, and Level-up Hackathons. We offer a hybrid workplace and a competitive compensation package, including pension according to ITP1, health allowance, parental leave top-up, and health care insurance.
   

  

**Your mission**
* Design and build scalable data pipelines for analytics and AI-driven features.
* Build on our existing lakehouse foundations to shape the architecture's next stage, balancing performance, flexibility, and cost.
* Ensure high data quality and reliability, implementing best practices in data modelling, governance and observability.
* Collaborate closely with our Data Analysts, Chief Architect, Engineers, and Product teams to create data-driven features and insights.
* Evaluate and implement new technologies to ensure a modern, scalable, and future-proof data stack.


**So, what are we looking for?**
* 5+ years of experience as a Data Engineer/Data Platform Engineer/Data Lead, working with large-scale data processing.
* Strong knowledge of cloud data platforms (Snowflake, Databricks, BigQuery, Azure, or AWS).
* Experience working with data lake and lakehouse technologies (cloud storage solutions, Iceberg tables, Delta Lake, etc.).
* Experience working with workflow orchestration platforms such as Prefect, Dagster, or Airflow, including designing, implementing, and operating data workflows in production environments.
* Experience with PostgreSQL, including data modeling, query optimization, and building reliable data movement patterns between operational and analytical systems.
* Experience with designing scalable architectures and pipelines that support large datasets efficiently.
* Passion for AI/ML-driven data features and enabling advanced analytics.
* Problem-solver mindset, you love optimizing performance and building robust systems.
* Curiosity & ownership – you thrive in an environment where you can experiment and improve.


**Not sure if you check all the boxes? Apply anyway!**
 We believe that mindset and passion are just as important as technical skills. If you have a software engineering background, love solving complex problems, and are a data nerd at heart, we’d love to hear from you, even if you don’t meet every requirement. We’re looking for the right person, not just the perfect resume. We are committed to building a diverse and inclusive team and welcome applications from candidates of all backgrounds, experiences, and abilities.
   

  

 Join us at Bannerflow and help us build a world-class data platform that powers the future of Ad Tech!

title: Azure Data Platform Engineer to Handelsbanken
description:
We are looking for a Cloud Data Platform Engineer to help us build Handelsbanken's future cloud-based Data Platform to meet the bank's future data & analytics needs. You will be part of a tight-knit, rapidly evolving team, building out one of the bank's most strategic platforms.
   

  

**Your Team**
 You will be part of a small team of platform engineers who are all passionate about building robust and scalable platforms. Your team is responsible for building and operating the technical foundations of the cloud data platform to enable data engineers from around the bank to build high-performant data pipelines and data products. The team is also responsible for building and maintaining the data ingestion pipelines to make business data available to data engineers.
   

  

**Your role**
 You must have hands-on experience deploying and configuring Azure Databricks workspaces - not just using Databricks for data engineering workloads. As a platform engineer your primary role will be focussed on building, configuring, scaling, operating and maintaining all of the components that comprise the Azure Data Platform, with a clear focus on Azure Databricks as the heart of the platform. In addition, you will also actively work with configuring various data integrations and data ingestion pipelines to make business data available to data engineers and end-users.
   

  

 Typical tasks that the role includes are:
   

  

* Deploying and configuring new Databricks workspaces, services and integrations using Infrastructure-as-Code (IaC)
* Creating and maintaining infrastructure build & release pipelines
* Continually optimising / automating routine tasks and procedures
* Ensuring that appropriate cost and platform monitoring capabilities are in place
* Building a professional network to foster effective cooperation with other areas of the IT-organisation where the team has dependencies (such as the IT security and Infrastructure provisioning divisions)
* Working closely with data engineers to secure optimised platform utilisation
* Configuring and enabling data integrations across a variety of patterns (API, file, events, CDC etc.) to support data ingestion into the platform
* Building and maintaining data ingestion pipelines
* Working actively to build capabilities to actively monitor the data quality of data coming into the data platform
* Actively participating in, and contributing to team planning ceremonies (stand-ups, backlog refinement, increment planning etc.)


 On top of your technical tasks, you will be expected to actively contribute with your knowledge, experience and personality in building a high-performing team.
   

  

**Your profile**
 We are looking for someone who has a demonstrable, "hands-on" track record of building, implementing, operating, and scaling an enterprise-scale cloud data platform built around Azure Databricks. This role focuses on platform engineering rather than data pipeline development. While familiarity with data engineering concepts is useful, the primary responsibility is building and operating the underlying Azure Databricks platform
   

  

 The technical skills we are looking for include:
   

  

* Deep knowledge and experience of designing and implementing enterprise-grade Azure Databricks platforms
* Good understanding of workspace architectures
* Strong experience in building and deploying cloud infrastructure across multiple environments using a Infrastructure as Code (IAC)
* Experience of working hands-on with cluster policies, pools, autoscaling strategies
* Strong experience of Azure IT security setup topics e.g. Private Link, networking design, Azure AD (Entra ID), RBAC, and managed identities
* Good experience of storage account architecture (ADLS Gen2 patterns)
* Good Azure cloud foundation skills (security, networking, encryption, access management etc.)
* Experience with data ingestion and data ingestion pipelines using various patterns (CDC, events, streaming, file, API etc.)
* Working knowledge of data engineering (DataOps) ways of working
* Knowledge, or experience, of using Microsoft Fabric or other similar SaaS data platforms is an advantage


 You are a proactive and solution-oriented individual who not only identifies opportunities for improvement but also takes ownership of implementing them. You have a strong service mindset and consistently put the customer first in everything you do. Additionally, you have a natural inclination to share knowledge and insights, both within your team and across organizational boundaries. You actively contribute to fostering a collaborative work environment where openness and teamwork are an integral part of everyday operations.
   

  

**We look forward to receiving your application!**

title: Cloud Solution Architect
description:
As of 2 September 2025, Vivicta, former Tietoevry Tech Services, now operates as a standalone company under our new brand. We’ve officially launched Vivicta, your trusted Nordic transformation partner, making everyday easier through technology. You can explore more about our brand and what we stand for at https://www.vivicta.com
   

  

 Ready to join us? Click ‘Apply’ and complete our short application form to get started. Prefer another option? Apply via LinkedIn to automatically populate your details using your profile.
   

  

 Brinner du för att omsätta modern teknik till verklig affärsnytta? I så fall är detta rollen för dig. Vi på Vivicta söker just nu en
 **Cloud Solution Architect** 
 som vill vara med och effektivisera våra kunders verksamheter! Hos oss får du arbeta med Azure, Power Platform och Dynamics 365 i en roll där du både får tänka strategiskt och vara hands-on i lösningarna.
   

  

 Som konsult hos oss blir du en teknisk specialist inom Azure, Power Platform och Microsoft Dynamics 365 Finance and Supply Chain. Du blir en del av ett växande team där arbetet präglas av frihet under ansvar och nära samarbete med kund.
   

  

**Som Cloud Solution Architect på Vivicta kommer du att**
* Arbeta nära kund i en konsultativ roll för att effektivisera deras verksamhet
* Driva implementationer, utbildningar och vidareutveckling av lösningar
* Hitta och implementera de bästa tekniska lösningarna för våra kunder
* Vägleda och stötta utvecklingsteam i tekniska frågor
* Hjälpa kunder att optimera sina system och miljöer
* Delta i både projektleveranser och applikationsförvaltning
* Bidra i dialoger med nya och befintliga kunder tillsammans med säljteamet
* Arbeta i projekt som ofta har internationell prägel, tillsammans med ett erfaret team och med möjlighet att påverka både lösningar och arbetssätt.


**Vi söker dig som**
* Har erfarenhet av Azure, Microsoft Power Platform och Dynamics 365 Finance and Supply Chain
* Har erfarenhet av integrationer mellan olika system
* Har arbetat med Microsofts utvecklingsverktyg, t.ex. Visual Studio och DevOps
* Är självgående, flexibel och resultatinriktad
* Har ett framåttänkande mindset och trivs i en agil miljö
* Har en stark vilja att utvecklas och hålla dig uppdaterad inom ny teknik
* Talar och skriver flytande svenska och engelska


**Det är meriterande om du också har**
* Djupare erfarenhet av Power Platform (t.ex. Logic Apps, Power Apps, Power BI, Dataverse)


**Vi erbjuder dig**
 Vi erbjuder dig professionell tillväxt, meningsfulla projekt, öppen kultur och en enastående balans mellan arbete och privatliv! Vi erbjuder dig en möjlighet att skapa framtiden för en snabbt växande och viktig sektor. Vi tror att vår organisationskultur är en viktig del för att du ska bli framgångsrik. Vi tillhandahåller en flexibel hybrid arbetsmodell som en del av vår kultur och vårt sätt att arbeta. Slutligen tror vi också på nyfikenhet och konstant lärande som en livsstil där du behöver skola om dig varje dag när nya möjligheter dyker upp.
   

  

**Hur du ansöker**
 Bifoga ditt CV eller LinkedIn-profil så kontaktar vi dig. Intervjuer kommer att hållas löpande, så vi uppmanar dig att söka redan idag. Om du har några frågor angående denna roll, vänligen kontakta anställande chef, Norman Fromy, norman.fromy@vivicta.com.
   

  

 Vi ser fram emot din ansökan!
   

  

**Plats:** 
 Stockholm, Göteborg, Malmö, Skellefteå/Hybrid
   

  

*Vi genomför bakgrundskontroller på alla slutkandidater, och vissa roller kan kräva säkerhetsprövning antingen under rekryteringen eller senare — alltid i enlighet med lokal lagstiftning samt med stor omsorg och konfidentialitet.*
*Vivicta tackar nej till samtal från rekryteringsföretag.*

title: Product Expert Network Security
description:
*Vill du vara med och framtidssäkra Länsförsäkringars nätverkssäkerhet? Som Product Expert – Network Security får du en nyckelroll i att utveckla våra hybrida nätverkstjänster, både on-prem och i publika moln. Du blir en del av Private Cloud Platforms, där vi driver framåtlutade initiativ för att skapa säkra, tillförlitliga och kostnadseffektiva lösningar som stöttar hela LF.*
**Om jobbet**
 Som Product Expert – Network Security får du en central roll i att säkerställa och utveckla våra nätverkssäkerhetstjänster inom området Private Cloud Platforms, som ingår i Development Solutions & Operation Platforms. Vårt uppdrag är att leverera tillgängliga, säkra och stabila hybrida IT-infrastrukturtjänster som möter både dagens och framtidens behov. Du arbetar nära Product Owner Network, IT-säkerhetsavdelningen samt andra interna och externa partners för att ta fram och realisera innovativa tekniska lösningar. Tillvaron är dynamisk och präglas av en pågående digital förflyttning, där du får stora möjligheter att påverka hur vi stärker våra nätverksleveranser och vår säkerhetsnivå. Rollen rapporterar till chefen för Private Cloud Platforms, medan den dagliga arbetsledningen sker av produktägaren för Network.
   

  

 Dina arbetsuppgifter kommer vara bland annat:
   

  

* Säkerställa och utveckla våra nätverkssäkerhetstjänster, både on-prem och i publika moln.
* Kravställa och följa upp mot externa partners och leverantörer.
* Hantera incidenter och problem.
* Driva kontinuerliga förbättringar och säkerställa kvaliteten på leveranserna.
* Erbjuda tekniskt stöd till interna konsumenter.
* Utveckla och implementera vår Product Road Map.


 Följande kommer göra dig framgångsrik i denna tjänst:
   

  

* Relevant kompetens inom området genom akademisk utbildning (t.ex. data-/systemvetenskap, civilingenjör) eller motsvarande arbetslivserfarenhet.
* God erfarenhet av infrastrukturell utveckling och teknisk kravhantering gentemot externa partners och leverantörer.
* Gedigen erfarenhet av drift och design av brandväggar, WAAP (Web Application and API Protection), DDoS-skydd, global lastbalansering samt proxy-tjänster från ledande leverantörer.
* Stark förmåga att driva mot en hög grad av automation i hanteringen av ovanstående plattformar.
* Kompetens och erfarenhet av loggning, monitorering och säkerhetsarbete, exempelvis härdning av hårdvara och mjukvara.
* Erfarenhet av riskhantering och arbete med säkerhetsramverk och tillhörande revisioner (t.ex. ISO27001, CIS, NIS/NIS2 och DORA).
* Vana att arbeta med förändringshantering och ramverk som ITIL och SAFe.
* Du har mycket god kommunikations- och presentationsförmåga och kan förmedla komplex information på ett enkelt och tydligt sätt, både på svenska och engelska


 Meriterande är även om du har:
   

  

* Erfarenhet av den operativa processen att designa och hantera en produkt eller tjänst under hela dess livscykel.
* Erfarenhet av att effektivisera ditt dagliga arbete med hjälp av AI-verktyg.


 Som person är du självgående och har förmågan att driva ditt arbete framåt självständigt, samtidigt som du är en lagspelare som stöttar och samarbetar nära med dina kollegor. Du har en stark helhetssyn som hjälper dig att nå både dina egna och teamets mål. Du trivs i en dynamisk och förändringsbenägen miljö, är strukturerad, kvalitetsmedveten och har ett genuint intresse för nätverkssäkerhet och moderna infrastrukturlösningar.
   

  

**Kontaktpersoner**
 Rekryterande chef: bjorn.fagerstedt@lansforsakringar.se
   

  

 Facklig representant Forena: jessica.loow@lansforsakringar.se
   

  

 Facklig representant Akademikerföreningen: lisa-maria.carensjo@lansforsakringar.se
   

  

*Låter det spännande? Då vill vi veta mer om dig! Välkommen med din ansökan redan idag. Vi arbetar löpande med denna rekrytering och kan komma att tillsätta tjänsten innan sista ansökningsdag.*
 Länsförsäkringar AB är en inkluderande arbetsplats där mod, möjligheter och engagemang står i centrum. Vi erbjuder ett hållbart arbetsliv med flexibla villkor och satsar på att alla medarbetare ska kunna växa och trivas, oavsett bakgrund.
   

  

 Digital & IT är hjärtat i Länsförsäkringsgruppens digitala utveckling. Vi driver den gemensamma digitala transformationen och levererar förstklassiga IT-lösningar, datadrivna insikter och innovativa digitala tjänster som skapar värde för hela verksamheten. Vårt uppdrag är att bygga en robust och framtidssäker IT-miljö – både på plats och i molnet – som möjliggör kundnära och effektiva affärer i hela gruppen.
   

  

 Hos oss möts teknik, affär och innovation. Vi arbetar tvärfunktionellt ute i verksamheten i agila team där utveckling, drift och strategi går hand i hand. Vi ansvarar för allt från IT-styrning, enterprisearkitektur och leverantörshantering till applikationsutveckling, cybersäkerhet och verksamhetsberedskap.

title: Lead Infrastructure Engineer
description:
Arcesium is a global financial technology firm that solves complex data-driven challenges faced by some of the world’s most sophisticated financial institutions. We constantly innovate our platform and capabilities to meet tomorrow’s challenges, anticipate the risks our clients encounter, and design advanced solutions to help our clients achieve transformational business outcomes.
 



  





 Financial technology is a high-growth industry as change and innovation continue to disrupt the status-quo and prompt major transformation. Arcesium is at a particularly interesting time in our own growth as we look to leverage our successfully established market position and expand operations in pursuit of strategic new business opportunities. We value intellectual curiosity, proactive ownership, and collaboration with colleagues, and we empower you to meaningfully contribute from day one and accelerate your professional development.
 



  





**Position Summary** 




 Arcesium seeks an accomplished Lead Infrastructure Engineer to join our Infrastructure team. All Infrastructure Engineers are coders and technology generalists who bring their unique combination of breadth and depth of experience and knowledge to the team. As a Lead Infrastructure Engineer, you’re passionate about providing the foundation and capabilities to our application developers to build upon, and love to solve problems with creative multi-discipline, polyglot, and/or open-source solutions. You are able to work independently, demonstrating “high agency” in your work, but you’ll balance your independence with leveraging the team and working with outside stakeholders as needed.
 



  





**What you will do:** 



* Engineer our Linux compute platform based on Kubernetes and AWS
* Engineer our Python and Terraform-based tools and automation
* Build infrastructure applications, write, and review code as needed
* Solve problems with a combination of coding and implementation of open source tools
* Troubleshoot and debug complex systems
* Leverage our design, coding, automation, and implementation skills to engineer what Arcesium needs, solving business and technology problems



  





**What you’ll need:** 



* We prefer 5+ years of relevant infrastructure experience. However, we are interested more in what you’ve done, can do, and can grow to do, rather than the number of years you’ve been working.
* Proven track record of ownership of critical systems and delivery of systems and features. You have owned and operated critical infrastructure systems like Linux, Kubernetes, DNS, Kerberos, and/or AWS IAM.
* Polyglot programming capability, with advanced level Python ability. Other languages in our ecosystem include Java, Go, and Bash.
* Experience with running critical workloads on AWS
* Ability to balance delivery with correctness, delivering timely solutions that are properly risk-managed for the needs of the business
* You have an inquisitive mind, the ability to learn and problem-solve, and are a self-starter
* A bachelor’s degree in computer science, computer engineering, or related discipline is required



  





 Arcesium's Personal Data Privacy Notice for Candidates is linked
 
 here
 
 .

title: DevOps Engineer
description:
*Kreditz is transforming how businesses assess credit and risk using real-time data and NextGen Credit Scoring solutions.*
*As one of Europe’s fastest-growing FinTech scaleups - we’ve earned a place on the* 
***Sifted 100: Nordics & Benelux***
*list in both 2024 and 2025 and most recently, we were proud to receive a* 
***double win at the Credit Management & Compliance Awards 2025***
*, being named both "* 
***Fintech Rocket of the Year***
*" and "* 
***Breakthrough of the Year***
*".*
*With strong growth across the Nordics and Europe, our clients range from leading banks and iGaming giants to fast-moving digital innovators.*
**About The Position**
 Kreditz is looking for a
 **DevOps Engineer** 
 to join our Tech team. In this role, you will build and evolve our cloud infrastructure, CI/CD pipelines, and platform tooling, ensuring our systems are scalable, reliable, and secure as we grow.
   

  

 This is a hands-on builder role for someone who enjoys automation, ownership, and turning complexity into clean, scalable infrastructure.
   

  

**What Can Kreditz Offer**
* A once-in-a-lifetime growth journey, from startup to Global scaleup
* A journey where everyone in the team plays a crucial role for our company’s success
* Fun-loving, driven, and passionate colleagues who support each other’s success
* A culture that celebrates progress, humility, and teamwork
* We win together
* 24/7 access to gym, sauna and sports facilities at our office, plus additional wellness allowance
* Competitive base salary


**RESPONSIBILITES**
**Some Of The Main Responsibilities You Will Cover**
* Design, build, and maintain cloud infrastructure using Infrastructure as Code (Terraform).
* Develop and optimize CI/CD pipelines for automated testing and deployment.
* Build and deploy services on AWS using ECS, Fargate, and Service Connect.
* Implement and maintain monitoring, alerting, and observability solutions.
* Collaborate with developers to improve deployment workflows and platform reliability.
* Work with serverless technologies (Lambda) for event processing and automation.
* Perform code reviews and contribute hands-on to infrastructure projects.
* Leverage AI agents and tools to enhance development workflows and productivity.
* Document infrastructure architecture, processes, and runbooks.


 Success means engineers ship confidently, systems scale without drama, and incidents are rare, visible, and quickly resolved.
   

  

**WE ARE LOOKING FOR**
**We Believe That You Have**
* A Bachelor's degree in Computer Science, IT, or related field (or equivalent experience).
* 5+ years of experience in DevOps, platform engineering, or backend development.
* Strong skills in Infrastructure as Code (Terraform).
* Experience with AWS services, particularly ECS, Fargate, and Service Connect.
* Experience with CI/CD tools and pipeline automation.
* Familiarity with containerization (Docker/Podman).
* Familiarity with message queuing (SQS, SNS).
* Experience with databases like PostgreSQL, MySQL, Redis, DynamoDB.
* Strong skills in monitoring and observability tools (CloudWatch, OpenTelemetry, X-Ray).
* Proficiency with Git and version control workflows.
* Scripting skills in Python, Bash, or Go.
* Excellent English communication skills (Swedish is a plus).
* Enthusiasm for using AI agents and tools in daily development work.
* Experience in FinTech is a plus.


**WHY JOIN NOW?**
 At Kreditz, you'll have the opportunity to work in a cutting-edge, fast-growing FinTech company that's redefining the credit scoring industry. With the backing of major investors and a clear vision for the future, your work will make a real impact on financial inclusion worldwide. Join us and be a part of something truly transformative!
   

  

**APPOINTMENT OF ROLE**
 Q1/Q2 2026
   

  

**SCOPE**
 Full-time, permanent employment (tillsvidareanställning)
   

  

**SALARY**
 Competitive salary
   

  

**REPORTING**
 You will be reporting to the company’s VP Engineering
   

  

**WORKPLACE**
 Danderyd, Stockholm
   

  

**RECRUITMENT PROCESS**
 Ongoing selection, we review candidates continuously.
   

  

*Last day for applications 31st March 2026*
**CRIMINAL RECORDS EXTRACT**
 As part of our recruitment process we will ask you to present a clean criminal records extract
   

  

**CONTACT**
 Lorenzo Puccio, VP Strategic Operations, Lorenzo@kreditz.com
   

  

 For more information about the role feel free to reach out to our Co-founder/CTO, arlen@kreditz.com
   

  

 Intelligent credit- & risk decisions made easy with open banking & AI-technology

title: Solutions Architect for Public Sector
description:
**Description**
 Are you passionate about cloud technology and helping customers innovate?
   

  

 Join our AWS Solutions Architecture team in Sweden and empower large enterprises across industries to innovate at speed and scale with cloud technologies.
   

  

 We are looking for a skilled, self-motivated technical leader to join a team of supportive, experienced professionals. As a Solutions Architect in the Public Sector team, you get to impact the lives of millions of citizens and help a variety of different customers - all the way from digital-native and government-linked companies to security-focused government agencies. Overall, the customers we work with include government agencies, state-owned companies, educational institutions, healthcare and non-profit organizations.
   

  

 Our Public Sector teams help customers build a deeper understanding of hyper-scale cloud technology and application architectures, and they have the consultative skills required to drive successful outcomes.
   

  

 Solutions Architects work hand in hand with AWS customers to help them make the most of the Cloud. They are at the crossroads of business and technology and engage with organizations at all stages of cloud adoption. Solutions Architects also take a leading role in creating and presenting technical content and best practices.
   

  

 Solutions Architects own the overall technical relationship between customers and AWS and make recommendations on security, cost, performance, reliability and operational efficiency. They work to understand the customer's business needs and give prescriptive guidance on how to create business value with technology. To do this they collaborate with other teams such as account management, professional services, support, product teams and the AWS partner ecosystem.
   

  

 In this role you will get to practice your creativity, linking technology to tangible solutions and educating AWS customers about the art of the possible. You will have the opportunity to define or invent cloud-native reference architectures for a variety of use cases. The Solutions Architecture team is a diverse group of technologists from a variety of backgrounds. Practical knowledge of the AWS platform is desired but not required, provided you have a sound technical foundation and a desire to learn.
   

  

 You will have the support to grow your expertise in industry and technology areas of depth. Every day you will learn something new from your customers, your peers and your own experiments.
   

  

 At Amazon you will be encouraged and rewarded for doing what is right for the long-term success of the customer. We value your passion to discover, invent and build on behalf of customers.
   

  

 If you can educate AWS customers about the art of the possible, while investigating the impossible, come build the future with us.
   

  

 Key job responsibilities
   

  

* As a Solutions Architect, you will work directly with customers to accelerate their projects and recommend best-practice architectures in line with their long-term business outcomes.
* You will own the technical relationship with the customer and operate as their trusted advisor. The best interests of the customer will shape the guidance you provide.
* Share the voice of the customer to influence the roadmap of new features and services for the AWS platform.
* Create and capture best practices, technical content and new reference architectures (e.g. white papers, code samples, blog posts).
* Evangelize and educate about AWS technology (e.g. workshops, user groups, meetups, public speaking, online videos or conferences).
* Contribute to the growth of the Solutions Architecture organization by hiring, coaching and mentoring others.
* Develop areas of depth in technical domains relevant to your interests and your customer's outcomes.


**About The Team**
 Diverse Experiences
   

  

 AWS values diverse experiences. Even if you do not meet all of the preferred qualifications and skills listed in the job description, we encourage candidates to apply. If your career is just starting, hasn’t followed a traditional path, or includes alternative experiences, don’t let it stop you from applying.
   

  

 Why AWS?
   

  

 Amazon Web Services (AWS) is the world’s most comprehensive and broadly adopted cloud platform. We pioneered cloud computing and never stopped innovating — that’s why customers from the most successful startups to Global 500 companies trust our robust suite of products and services to power their businesses.
   

  

 Inclusive Team Culture
   

  

 AWS values curiosity and connection. Our employee-led and company-sponsored affinity groups promote inclusion and empower our people to take pride in what makes us unique. Our inclusion events foster stronger, more collaborative teams. Our continual innovation is fueled by the bold ideas, fresh perspectives, and passionate voices our teams bring to everything we do.
   

  

 Mentorship & Career Growth
   

  

 We’re continuously raising our performance bar as we strive to become Earth’s Best Employer. That’s why you’ll find endless knowledge-sharing, mentorship and other career-advancing resources here to help you develop into a better-rounded professional.
   

  

 Work/Life Balance
   

  

 We value work-life harmony. Achieving success at work should never come at the expense of sacrifices at home, which is why we strive for flexibility as part of our working culture. When we feel supported in the workplace and at home, there’s nothing we can’t achieve.
   

  

**Basic Qualifications**
* Technical Degree (Computer Science, Maths, Engineering or equivalent) and/or relevant tech experience
* Fluent written and verbal communication skills in English
* Experience of Software, Systems or Data Design
* Knowledge of a modern programming language (Python, JavaScript, Go, .Net, Java, etc.)
* Passion for technology and for learning


**Preferred Qualifications**
* Experience designing, building, refactoring or operating large scale and impactful IT projects - either on premises or in the cloud
* Working knowledge of cloud native architectures
* In depth working knowledge in a technology domain such as distributed internet-scale web or mobile applications, DevOps, Serverless, Big Data, analytics, Machine Learning, enterprise workloads (SAP, VMware, Windows etc.), high-performance databases (SQL and/or NoSQL), complex networking implementations, highly secured workloads etc.
* Knowledge of modern data processing techniques and systems
* Experience working in a customer-facing role
* Experience collaborating effectively with internal or external stakeholder groups at multiple levels
* AWS certification (e.g. AWS Solutions Architect Associate or Professional)
* Continued or advanced learning (Master, MBA, PhD or equivalent)


 Amazon is an equal opportunities employer. We believe passionately that employing a diverse workforce is central to our success. We make recruiting decisions based on your experience and skills. We value your passion to discover, invent, simplify and build. Protecting your privacy and the security of your data is a longstanding top priority for Amazon. Please consult our Privacy Notice (https://www.amazon.jobs/en/privacy\_page) to know more about how we collect, use and transfer the personal data of our candidates.
   

  

 Our inclusive culture empowers Amazonians to deliver the best results for our customers. If you have a disability and need a workplace accommodation or adjustment during the application and hiring process, including support for the interview or onboarding process, please visit https://amazon.jobs/content/en/how-we-hire/accommodations for more information. If the country/region you’re applying in isn’t listed, please contact your Recruiting Partner.

 - AWS EMEA SARL (Sweden Branch)
